title: Springtown Blues
description: A fellow deserving of the title "singer/songwriter" in the most positive of ways. Not just another schmuck with an acoustic guitar. Beautiful songs, beautiful lyrics, beautiful voice. An all around beautiful guy!

title: Bruno is Orange
description: The first time I saw Frances perform I was absolutely floored. I can't really comprehend how her voice can be as incredible as it is, it's somewhat reminiscent of the singer of the Cranberries. She writes fantastic songs and is one of the best "solo artists" going.

title: Farmingvillians
description: One of the most righteous party bands playing badass gruff punk you'll see. Perpetually on hiatus, but perpetually writing, recording, and playing shows. Doing rad stuff for the Long Island music community. Dead Broke Rekerds/Kiss of Death!

title: Circle to Shreds
description: B&T outside in the sun. Pat Schramm definitely needs some color. Google "Pat Schramm" to see an outtake from this show on YouTube.

title: les pleins succes
description: Williamsburg at the Woodser. The drummer also has an acoustic act that goes by the name francois virot.

title: Underground Husbands
description: At the Woodser in Williamburg, NY. Kickball is one of my favorite bands and one of the first on IYMI.

title: Im from Kingston
description: Three piece Pop punk band a la early 90's East Bay. This is their hit homage to their old stomping grounds, Kingston, NY. Performed at the legendary Death Trap, 29 Custer Street in Buffalo!

title: The Origamists
description: Lemuria at bereafest. It got kind of late and the video got more and more orange. People love the lemuria sing along, I guess it helps with songs as catchy as this.

title: Until the Day I Die
description: Double camera action of Slingshot at bereafest. Catch Phil Douglas camped out in the back right.

title: Rubrics
description: New song from their upcoming record "East/West", coming out on No Idea records. If you didn't know, Jeff is a teacher.

title: What We Were
description: Ryan of Delay & Lisa brewing up a dose of acoustic pop, positivity, and nostalgia in their hometown of Berea, OH.

title: Read Side For Details
description: Ghost Town Trio playing "Read Side For Details" at Berea Fest in Berea, OH, featuring Matty Oconke of Tin Armor.

title: Pleading Songs
description: Good buddies from Athens, GA, play some awesome pop at Bereafest. Christopher is without his liver right now.

title: Ballpoint Pen
description: Halo Fauna live with "Ballpoint Pen." A quiet crowd, but Christopher was into it, and that counts for something.

title: Boutros understands me
description: Awesome bros from way back allentown ways, with baby G-wacks, Jon/Cody on guitar. For fans of love, puppies and dumpstered vegan items. This is from Bereafest 07.

title: Everyone Does Everything
description: The drummer of Michigan's Abe Froman going solo. This man can play the guitar unlike most any other humans. He can also write a beautiful song that can make you smile or another one that will make you cry. An under appreciated musician, for sure.

title: My True Step
description: Tin Armor from way back in the room at Bereafest. This is off their full length on One Percent Press. Also check out their pink couch session below.

title: Too Long For A Headstone
description: These dudes jump, these dudes scream, these dudes play some really infectious pop-punk songs and live lives as positive and up-beat as their tunes that get stuck in you head.
The mothers in Greenpoint, NY. This show was supposed to be on the roof, however torrential rain canceled that.

title: the Worm of War
description: Dante Cimadamore solo and live in the early If You Make It days. I made up the title "the Worm of War" myself; he never gave it one.

title: The Small Arms Psalms Dealer Suite
description: Featuring Phil Douglas on the skins, this was filmed in Holbrook NY at Union Square Bar.

title: Say Fiesta
description: Incredible singer from France, who also plays drums for Clara Clara. Multi-talented to say the least. This is from a show at Williamsburg's The Woodser with Kickball and Clara Clara.

title: Waiting for Things
description: Brooklyn - France globetrotter. Hauntingly beautiful music.

title: I Will Always Know What $0.99 Can Get Me
description: Potboiler in a video montage cut from a couple of their shows, and one of my first real stabs at editing. Rough, fast, and fun.

title: Workers
description: Frances in New Paltz. Beautiful greens and amazing people. Awesome sing along too.

title: How Could Anyone Understand a Turtle?
description: Filmed at 2007's Bereafest, you can't hear Sheena, but everyone singing along definitely evens that out.

title: Shoulders
description: Kickball doing "Shoulders." This band can do no wrong, and I clearly can't stop posting them. Live, 2007.

title: N/A
description: Each Other's Mothers, amazingly composed and dancy math-rock that's tighter than it has any right to be. Live, 2007.

title: Brown Brown Brown
description: Paul Baribeau playing "Brown Brown Brown" at Stay True Paltz Part Deux. It was such a beautiful day out you can almost feel it on camera.

title: Boy Meets Girl
description: Ghost Mice playing "Boy Meets Girl" to a reverse circle pit of punks, the whole room facing out instead of in.

title: Stay True Paltz
description: Featuring Eric of Halo Fauna.

title: Jumpcut
description: Get to the Point, the Funtime Entertainment series with Jeff holding forth on the finer points of life. This one: "Jumpcut."

title: Fieldtrip
description: Funtime Entertainment presents "Fieldtrip," from the Get to the Point series with Jeff. Small adventures, narrated with a straight face.

title: Location Location Location
description: Talent sprawled all over the town of New Paltz. Amazing people playing equally amazing, technical, mathy rock. Check for releases on No Idea.

title: The First Communication
description: Instore perfomance at Curmudgeon. From their upcoming DVD.

title: My True Step
description: Berea loves them some Tin Armor. I couldn't get close enough to film it properly, but it sounds really great. They just added Matt U's brother John to the band.

title: Christmas Lights
description: Paul at Stay True Paltz. I spliced in some fake behind the back video.

title: Illies
description: Two Camera shot from Stay True Paltz. Featuring Jeff of Funtime and Eric of Halo Fauna.

title: Figure 8
description: Ghost Mice playing "Figure 8" at the Stay True Paltz! festival, circle pit swirling with the band right in the middle of it.

title: Ohio
description: A shout out from Tom & Carly to their friends doing really incredible stuff for the DIY music scene in Ohio.

title: Watertowers
description: Delay at Stay True Paltz. These three are my favorite. If you are ever in Columbus, OH look them up. They live at the monster house.

title: Chuckie
description: Singer/guitarist of Halo Fauna playing a song adapted from one of his grandfather's poems. He has a full CD of song's written this way called Odds & Ends. Get it from him directly or from Plan-it-x.com.

title: N.O.J.O.B as in "ain't no Jay or bee"
description: N.E.D. at Stay True Paltz, from France, on tour with Ghost Mice. Really tight, Really energetic instrumental songs along the lines of Don Caballero.

title: This House
description: Jordaan Mason performing "This House" at the Stay True Paltz! festival in New Paltz, NY.

title: marche aux puces
description: L'hiver at Stay True Paltz. Members of Laser Awesome. A french influenced, mostly instrumental, harmony of eclectic songwriting.

title: Cars
description: Funtime Entertainment presents "Cars," another short from the Get to the Point series with Jeff. Equal parts deadpan and sincere.

title: Amphibian
description: Laura Stevenson & the Cans on the Pink Couch for "Amphibian," with Mike and Alex. The very first Pink Couch Session ever filmed.

title: Kaboom
description: supah' Bobby on the Pink Couch. Jeff also runs Funtime Entertainment and does some of the comedy videos on this site.

title: Showdown in Slalom City
description: Shadowmaps in Brooklyn.

title: Greasetruck Sandwich
description: Vietnam Werewolf in Berea.

title: Wonderstuff
description: The Steal (UK) at the Charleston. They covered the Beastie Boys this night.

title: We Fight Systems
description: Dynamite Arrows playing "We Fight Systems" at the Charleston in Brooklyn. Dan Edelman is an awesome guy, and the set proves it.

title: Daguerreotype
description: TMK at 99 Custer St. (The Last Stand) in Buffalo, NY. Featuring members of Halo Fauna and Failure's Union.

title: Oh, Susquehanna!
description: Defiance, Ohio at 29 Custer Street, or the Death Trap. Great close-ups from Eric on this one. Kids love D,O.

title: Nothing to Say
description: Paul in Buffalo. You can buy his records, including his recent Grand Ledge, from Plan-it-x.

title: Why we bike at night
description: Jono at 99 Custer Street in Buffalo. You can check him out under the name Grandfather Clock.

title: Turn Your Back
description: Sex and Hosses at Curmudgeon, from their upcoming dvd. Hopefully we'll have more clips soon.

title: S.O.S.
description: Somehow this band is able to combine the most poppy music with the most depressing, self-loathing, suicide-reference-laced lyrics ever and make it work, ridiculously well. It's difficult to avoid a comparison to fellow Minneapolis pop punk stalwarts, Dillinger Four.

title: Cannonball
description: Fake Problems in Buffalo. The sound isn't too great, but you can feel the INTENSITY.

title: Phone Number
description: New videos from Jeff at Funtime Entertainment. Listen for this number at the end of the clip.

title: Mrs. Worthington
description: Andy Cook playing "Mrs. Worthington" in Berea, OH, recorded by Dominic and Eric on a trip out to Ohio.

title: Memories
description: Jeff of Funtime Entertainment for the Get to the Point series, this time on the topic of memory. Short, dry, and oddly moving.

title: And By Queen
description: Dave at Stay True Paltz. He has a number of great records which you can listen to on his myspace.

title: Bad Mood
description: Cheeky at Serpent Fest. This video is badly lit, but the audio actually came out a lot better than I thought. Hopefully we can get more footage of this great band soon. You can buy/download their newest release from Quote/Unquote Records.

title: Our War with Faith
description: Mike and Jim on the pink couch. For fans of Hot Water Music and Rumbleseat. We had Jeff on ichat watching on too. The 2nd song of the series.

title: Mr. and Mrs. Everyone
description: Eric is the singer of New York's Halo Fauna. He has a cd of his grandfather's poems, which you can buy from Plan-It-X.

title: No Time and No Age
description: Mike and Jim on the pink couch. For fans of Hot Water Music and Rumbleseat. We had Jeff on ichat watching on too. The 1st song of the series.

title: The Golden Ghost
description: Slingshot at Bereafest. This is a 2 camera video, with the second camera provided by our own Eric Ayotte.

title: Fight
description: Kickball tearing through "Fight" in Williamsburg, Brooklyn. One of my favorite bands of this era, and one I could never stop filming.

title: Scarlet Rose
description: Andy Cook of Ghost Town Trio on the Pink Couch. This was filmed at 11am, after a long night in Ridgewood. Andy and the rest of the Ghost Town Trio crew came over, we ate some bagels and recorded two amazing acoustic songs. Thanks to Jiro for driving and K

title: Pajammin
description: Lexington, KY at the Shrieking Shack. This song is off of their demo ep which you can buy from them. They are recording soon, so check up on them.

title: Sadder Weirder
description: After a long night of 22's, Jeff decided that tonight would be the night we record his Pink Couch. There are a lot of funny clips from this recording, but in the essence of time I cut them out. Wait for the DVD for more, and a Regina Spektor song.

title: Changing the Wheel
description: White York in Bloomington, Indiana. The lighting was amazing here, although my camera made it a little dark. This is the A-side off of their recent 7in.

title: The Ghost Intrigue
description: This is from a recent mid-week show at Fontanas. Not that many people came out for this but they definitely missed a great show. They have a recent release, Unsettled, on Red Leader.

title: Friends in Jail
description: The always great 99 Custer St basement in Buffalo. The Failures Union is a mid-90's influenced indie rock band from Buffalo, NY. It features members of Lemuria, The Exit Strategy, and The Grade Grubbers. Their album "Sinker" out now on ONE PERCENT PRESS.

title: Get Outta Here
description: Some of my favorite people. In between two long nights at Lost&Found, Kate and Angie managed to stop over and record two songs. They have an EP out on quoteunquote, which you can download for free, or donate money towards.

title: Homage to the Tumbleweed
description: The Fellow Project play insanely memorable folk punk rock. Folk inspired punk rock with elements of The Modern Lovers and lyrical gusto of Lou Reed scattered about. (roklok). The have a new album on Dead Broke Rekerds.

title: The Past Few Years
description: Andy and Jiro are 2/3 of the band. They have are recent 4 way split with Potboiler, Down in the Dumps, and Fellow Project. Plus another split with Traffic and Weather. They also drank 2 dollar bottles of wine.

title: untitled
description: Best part about breaking down in Gainesville. My brother got me into these guys, and they randomly played a show the same day I was there. Eric, Dom and I were blown away with how tight they were. So good and technically astounding.

title: I Haven't Been Myself This Past Decade
description: Brother and sister duo take it to the pink couch. They have an EP coming out very very soon. I also play drums in this band.

title: The Going Gets Weird
description: Superband featuring members of Bomb the Music Industry!, The Fad and Latterman. Sounds exactly like a drunk irish pub band, including the Irish Car Bombs between songs.

title: Rainbow Connection
description: A special Valentine's treat from Laura and Dave. Dave plays in Chicago's Maps and Atlases, and was visiting during the week. We have more songs from him and Laura coming up soon.

title: The Diet of Worms
description: Matt and John Umland are brothers, who sound like cousins. They are also in the Ohio band Tin Armor. For fans of Ted Leo and The Shins.

title: Desperado Plunge
description: From our show at Gainesville's Wayward Council. We have a new record out on Plan-It-X too. This is the last song on the record, Durak.

title: Spontaneous Combustion
description: Awesome poppy punky quartet from Gainesville. Featuring the main man, Chris Clavin, of Ghost Mice & Plan-it-X fame. This is from a semi-recent Wayward Council show.

title: Lightning Storm
description: Super energetic punk from Ohio. This is off their semi-recent full length "Don't Laugh". These 3 are some of the best people out there, so catch them next time they are on tour, or whenever you are in Columbus.

title: Heavy Heart of Gold
description: "Cynthia Schemmer is a Long Island native who faked hating pizza for years. She now lives life as if it's Spring Break at Senor Frogs 24/7 and refuses to cover Saves The Day anymore." -by Micah

title: ScrewHead
description: Reminds of a cross between Hot Hot Heat and The Velvet Teen, but I'm mostly basing that on the vocals. From FontanasNYC. I think I'm going to start filming more shows here, since there is no problem with lighting or sound.

title: A Deuce Cornucopia
description: From one of their Chattanooga "Tests of Endurance". They are supposed to play until everyone leaves, but with everyone drunk, that wasn't happening.

title: Artichokes
description: From their recent NYU show with The National and Phosphorescent. Finger tappy madness. Some compare them to Hella or Don Caballero. Pink Couch coming soon.

title: Addressing Numbness
description: Hannah from Ghost Mice's new band, with some great Bloomington locals. Reminds me of a low key modest mouse.

title: Witch
description: Maps and Atlases' last day staying at our quaint Clinton Hill B&B. This is following up their recent 3 show appearance in the big apple. Hopefully they all come back soon. Thanks to Mike Campbell for filming while I was at work.

title: Thus Spoke Jeff Daniels
description: Another video of Craig and Julia playing with only half a band. This is a new song, but I guess they are all new songs if there isn't any recordings. I think this song reminds of Rilo Kiley, but I just woke up. I think it sounds great though, and makes me kinda sad.

title: Lake-A-Flake
description: From his recent tour with Eric Ayotte and Paul Baribeau. This is from multiple shows across tour, so you can check out the locals while you listen to Eric and Jeff spit hot fire.

title: Happy Hour
description: First installment of the 31 Movies, Team North. Featuring all-stars Eric Ayotte, Jeff Ledellaytner, and Domonic \[sic\] Armao. Check back every tuesday for more. Team South coming soon too.

title: Gone Gone Gone
description: From their Art of the Underground single series. The whole gang was here for some late night chinese food plus tons of bagels. They are on tour right now with Eric Ayotte, who is all over this site. The mp3 will be posted as soon as the single series is in distribution.

title: Life I've Got
description: From Hannah's amazing basement in Bloomington, IL. Austin has a split coming out soon with Hot Water Music's Chuck Ragan. He mesmerized Dominic during this show, who proclaimed Austin his favorite.

title: Eye got a feelin'
description: From Lost and Found, Andy just made up this title I think. Tracking down the song names is usually the hardest part. For fans of Ted Leo and his ilk.. Extra bouncy.

title: The State of Things
description: Tin Armor minus 2. It's off of their new EP on Whoa Oh Records. They are also playing Otto's Shrunken Head on St. Patrick's Day with Annabel who just finished their PC too.

title: New Paltz Summer
description: It may not look like it, but I'm pretty out of it too. You can pretty much infer our state by the bottles duct taped to eric's hands. Dom is in top form.

title: Won't you sleep with me tonight
description: Jono was in the area recently, with buddies from his other band Straight, No Chaser. I've been a big fan of his music ever since I saw Dominic's footage from his buffalo show. For fans of Owen and his kinsella brethren.

title: One Love
description: One of the funniest skits of all time in my honest opinion. Bob Marley would be proud. Sorry about the late posting on this, I need to get a better schedule going.

title: The Coney Island Song
description: Good buddy Frances on the pink couch. This is the new updated version, with better lighting, and longer hair. She's working on a full length, which should be coming out soon.

title: Futility and Familiarity
description: One of our last and best shows on tour with White York, although they didn't play this night. This was at 99 Custer Street in Buffalo, and it was great to see all our friends from up there.

title: Life
description: From the Laila Lounge in williamsburg. This is the last song I put up featuring Eric, unless he picks up the electric violin or alto sax.

title: Wayward Kites
description: Another Lost and Found show, one of many that I journeyed to that week. This a new unreleased song by the guys, I actually had to force them to name it last night.

title: Paul Baribeau goes to get Ice Cream
description: Norelle reminded me that I said I would put these up every tuesday. So thank her for reminding me at the last minute. Here's another video featuring Paul Baribeau.

title: Premonition
description: Another piece from the collection, again featuring Paul Baribeau. Eric discovers a new found power, will he use it for good or evil? Watch to find out.

title: Beloved Cyst
description: This is off of their extremely new release 'Census', on The Tone Library. Extremely jazzy, spazzy and wirh a crazy driving push to it. For fans of At The Drive In and that breed.

title: All We Have
description: Fierce Brosnan in New Brunswick, NJ. This show was for Randy Reddell's birthday. Madness ensued and brought two amps to their knees during their set, one on my brother's foot and another face-planted on the cement. From what I hear, this band has been deaded. So enjoy it while you can.

title: Parade Rest
description: A welcome addition to the pink couch. Recently on tour with Tin Armor, they were able to jump in and record as soon as TA quit farting around. I have live footage of them coming up soon. Check out the sweet vocal harmonies at the end, and enjoy my reversal in the beginning.

title: Songs For Ghosts To Haunt To
description: I didn't think that they would post this before I put it on the site, but boy I was wrong. Oh well, in the interest of letting other people see this, I will push this to the front page. Enjoy the technical madness of Maps and Atlases.

title: Stone and Halo
description: This is actually two songs sequentially named Stone and Halo I & II, off of their album Colony Collapse. Chris Enriquez gives me drum sadness. These are 3 of the most talented dudes in new york today.

title: 911 Girlfriend
description: Another installment from Team North, this time featuring Norelle and some of the Boston team of Spot Media.

title: The Right Girl
description: From Matchless in Brooklyn. An all star reincarnation of the Modern Machines, featuring Kate Eldridge of Cheeky and Mikey Erg. In this live video they ask the eternal question of "Who do you love"?

title: Crazy Bananas
description: This isn't the name of the song....but I've decided to take some creative liberties, since they never name any of their songs. If anyone comes up with one, let me know and I can change it right away. This is from the Encrypt record release show.

title: Finders Keepers
description: "Finders Keepers," a 31 Movies short featuring Dominic and Jono. It's so hard to be a good Samaritan these days.

title: Cost Benefit
description: I never heard of this band until this show, but I'm glad that I got the chance. This is from the Encrypt Manuscript release show. Reminiscent of a math-ier Karate.

title: Widow Party
description: This is from their recent tour with Tin Armor. You can also watch their pink couch session which we recorded while they were staying here for the weekend.

title: Keepsakes
description: Another song that I was never able to find the title for. A great band from Richmond, VA, with semi-new addition Erin Tobey.

title: Dead
description: The first from Team South, which includes members of Beat TV and Plan-it-X records of Gainesville, Florida. This video is from their Zombie Shorts catalogue.

title: Unlimited Breadsticks, Soup and Salad Days
description: I don't think anyone was happy about the venue being at the Sullivan Hall, but I think everyone ended up having a bunch of fun. The sound check took forever for BTMI, but it didn't even matter because nothing was mixed at all. At least you can hear Jeff.

title: i can't stay
description: Next door neighbors make the trek over to pink couch country. They recently played SXSW acoustically, and decided to give it another try back home. They are playing a ton of shows around New York, so make sure to check them out.

title: Marshing My Mellow
description: This short from team north is full of one liners and quotable lines. This also features another appearance by acting great Paul Baribeau. Eric and Dom also star.

title: Let be honest
description: They recently released a split with Brooklyn's own Dynamite Arrows, which you should definitely check out. For fans of Ultra Dolphins.

title: West Wind Ride
description: One of my favorite bands at the moment, and I'm excited to be able to play with them again on June 1st. They have a brand spankin' new full length called "Into Lake Griffy". So so so so so good. Seriously.

title: Nervous Rex
description: 3 different takes, 2 guitars, and one missing tape later, we finally have a new Laura Stevenson song. Miss having her around all the time. We had a bunch of funny outtakes from this session. Hopefully I remember them for the DVD.

title: Two Mice
description: Another video from their record release show of "Census". I've also decided to follow the format of the pink couch videos and add a little intro piece to each video. Enjoy.

title: Xrays
description: Dischord inspired rock from snowy Buffalo. Check out their recent release on One Percent Press, "City of Microphones". Tony from the Failure's Union is also in this band.

title: The Rabbits Are Back
description: A side project from Jason Anderson. Catchy and infectious, you can't help but smile and sing along.

title: Common Mistake
description: The horror! The horror! Sorry for the lapse in 31s. I promise to never leave you again, and this time it's for real. I swear it. Enjoy this spoooooky moooovie.

title: The Moon Will Rise
description: From their recent tour with Eric Ayotte, who I can't even get off the website if I tried. Sorry about all the people talking, it happens sometimes.

title: Fatigued
description: A ten hut. A true war story which inspired Full Metal Jacket, Apocalypse Now and Platoon.

title: I Miss that Band
description: Paul in Gainesville. It's been a while since we had any paul footage here. Hopefully we can get him to come up to new york soon and record a pink couch. Thanks to Jeff L for the video.

title: Her First Day
description: Kristina was able to get a stray kitten from our neighborhood. She's in my room right now getting used to the surroundings and it's off to the vet tomorrow. This the cheesiest video in the world, song by The Elected. Check back for new episodes.

title: Gift Horse
description: Witches at the Cake Shop. Cara Beth also recorded a Witches Pink Couch, which I will post first thing next week. Hopefully this band will have some recordings soon, but in the meantime, check out cara beth's myspace @ http://www.myspace.com/carabethsatalino.

title: Boys Night In
description: Another boring night in Buffalo.....or is it? Thanks to Sheena, Alex, and Pat for letting them run amok for a month. Look out for the baby monkey poster in a bunch of these movies, and the telltale orange walls.

title: The Center
description: Cara Beth from Witches came over and enjoyed some bergen bagels and coffee with a couple of buddies. Witches also feature long island's native son Clancy, and wandering bass-man Jared. It's Witches week!

title: Ginsburg
description: Nana Grizol at the cake shop in Manhattan. This show was tons of fun, plus I think the video came out really well. Thanks to Matte for the song name.

title: Bringing Them Back To Life
description: Good buddies Good Luck had the pleasure of living in "luxury" in brookly recently. I figured they could pay me back by recording a pink couch. Stay tuned for a Matty PC solo Pink Couch too.

title: Illustrations
description: Good buddies Phil and Lisa recently came down to live the brooklyn dream, which includes sweating out the oldies in a smelly apartment. This is also the debut of the new HD camera I just bought, so hopefully it looks a little cleaner. Big Boy did it.

title: Some Kind of Cadwallader
description: Every June, New York has Make Music Day, where they open up the city parks and streets to musicians. Algernon and a bunch of other great bands played. Pink Couch coming in the future.

title: at once and for all of us
description: Matt came over a while ago and recorded this epic 12 minute song. It took me a while to figure out the logistics of getting it up, and I finally figured it out. So sit back and enjoy Wildebeest, straight out the pink couch. That sounded dorky.

title: Unicorn Song
description: Dave came over and recorded with second banana Dom, since I was away on some dorky family vacation. He got lost in the rain for a while, but finally found his way here. I'm grateful that we were able to work something out, despite not being able to be there. Enjoy.

title: Waiting Room
description: Make Music is this amazing day in June, where the boroughs open up there parks and streets to music. Slingshot decided to debut their new cover of Fugazi's "Waiting Room", so check it out.

title: Hoods Up
description: From Make Music Day in Brooklyn, featuring original singer John Galm. My brother has been singing recently, but due to some recent unfortunate incidents, could'nt make it. John got pretty dirty, but luckily left his sandals on the sidelines.

title: Sense, Sensibility
description: Somehow we found the time and energy to travel from Hamden, CT back to brooklyn and record this at 2:30 in the morning. Thanks to Sean and Ben for trekking out here, if you missed them on the Asian Man tour, you're probably already regretting it. This is a new song, freshly named.

title: Anaesthesia
description: Finishing off the Make Music Three is Young Hearts, who I've seen a bunch of times but always failed to record something. Well not this time! They have a new EP called "The Fight EP" which you can download or buy online at their site.

title: Under the Sea
description: One of the best renditions of a classic disney song out there. Matty does it justice by preserving the integrity and heart of the original. Enjoy the finest pink couch session yet.

title: Pants
description: Buffalo heartthrobs finally made their way down to Brooklyn for a series of shows capping off the Asian Man records tour. If you haven't seen them yet, you've definitely missed your chance, because they've been out on the road for a while. This is off their recent full length "Get Better".

title: A horse that races or a racing horse?
description: We finally figured out a time to get the gang over here, although it got so hot that Joe was starting to glisten. This is a brand new song, but you should also check out there last full length on dead broke, "The Buried Life".

title: Cry-Baby
description: Featuring members of Latterman, Jonesin, and Small Arms Dealer. The "ungh" you hear in the beginning is from Mike Vlad. You can download their 5 song demo at their website which is linked below.

title: Books About Miles Davis
description: The Ergs! joined on stage by Jay and Alex from Lemuria. Unfortunately I wasn't able to tape them at the knitting factory show. Thankfully they also played this show at Hamilton Lanes in Trenton. They have a whole mess of records that you probably own already.

title: Call to the Comptroller's Office
description: Pat couldn't make it out for this taping, so we were able to squeeze in an upright bass in his place. This is the video that prompted me to upgrade my microphone system, since Jeff was able to peak the audio each time with my new camera. Luckily I have a backup.

title: Curly's Gold
description: From Halo Fauna's tour stop in Detroit. Marco of TandW put us up and was all around great friend in setting up this show (bobby too.). You can hear more on their recent split with Red and Blue on Mandible Records.

title: Stab yer Dad
description: This is a new Spoonboy song that you may or may not have heard already. He was on tour right before I left, and I'm really glad we able to get him over here. He also plays in the Max Levine Ensemble.

title: Bloodpact
description: Twin sisters, Ellie and Louise from Ireland, recently toured with Ghost Mice all over the country before heading home yesterday. This was filmed in Bloomington, IN. Check out their full length on Plan-It-X.

title: Hey Matt
description: Good Luck in Bloomington, IN. Matt, Ginger and Mike are three of the nicest people you'll meet and one of my favorite bands right now. This is from a weird show they played for a "block party" which consisted of 6 tents and legos. This has a different drum part than the original.

title: Dummies
description: One of the first recordings done for IYMI, Francois returns to the US to tour with Ramona Cordova. Between van troubles and sickness, I was happy to have him over before his flight home. Francois also drums for the French band Clara Clara.

title: Blame a Bird For Your Shortcomings
description: Although it took forever to record, we finally did it. yay. Should have been much easier considering two of the people live with the IYMI equipment 24/7. This is off our latest release, Durak, on Plan-it-X. I now understand how awkward this whole project is.

title: Biggest Blackout in US History
description: The Brass and Halo Fauna recently played a show together. At the last minute we decided to have an impromptu reunion set with no practice, which is pretty obvious by the sound of it. If you want the 4 song demo you can download it from LAST.FM or myspace. EPIC FAIL

title: Endless Mountain Valley
description: Rosendale local and the Kiss Ups drummer recently came down to visit, and to be the guinea pig out for the new mic setup. He has a full length record on Art of the Underground records which you should definitely check out.

title: Handpocket
description: At the last minute I realized that these three would be in town. Luckily I was able to track them down and get them to stop over for a pink couch, which will be up soon. This is from their show at Death By Audio. Super fun, dancey, cowbelly fun.

title: Ridgewood
description: Detroit, MI and Ridgewood, NY combine in a whirlwind of sound and fury. They came to new york, played and conquered. Check out their full length on Salinas Records.

title: Hair Pool
description: The new Brady Bunch came over after some last minute emails. They played a show with good buddies the State Lottery, who have IYMI stuff coming soon too. I'm pretty sure this is the most we've had on the couch so far. Stay tuned for a deluge of content.

title: Circus Song
description: We were able to wrangle them over to record at the last possible second. This video has some of the best use of minimal drums yet on the couch, plus everyone loves cowbell. Buy their full length on PIX while you are at it.

title: Cities We're Not From
description: In honor of the Don's birthday, I'm going to let the state lottery pink couch out of it's holding cell. Unfortunately he's not in the recording at all. This is the title track off of their recent record on Salinas Records.

title: Holland, 1945
description: From the Asian Man tour. Jeff from Bomb invited Sean from Andrew Jackson Jihad to come back on stage and sing this cover of this Neutral Milk Hotel song. Expect more from this show, including footage of the queers, kepi ghoulie, lemuria and ajj.

title: Hard Work
description: Paul at this year's Bereafest. I'm just starting to go through all the footage. Some stuff turned out great, and some came out kind of muddy. I'll probably get the acoustic stuff up first and work on the electric some more. Paul is going to be on tour with good buddies Good Luck.

title: Three Ghosts
description: Good buddies from Louisville, KY, who I've been trying to get on tape correctly for a while. I love everything about this band, great people and amazing skills. Drummer Ben also does amazing artwork, which is on about a quarter of the attendants to this year's Bereafest.

title: Alameda
description: Dom stopped by recently after playing a show with Hop Along for this year's Big She-Bang. He has a 7inch coming out on Deadbroke Rekerds and Leatherfist Records. Frances also joins him on this recording.

title: 5 to 9
description: On tour from Richmond, TRB was in town to play a show with 'labelmates' Bomb the Music Industry. I think they set the land-speed record for filming a pink couch, only taking 1 take on each song. They are on tour now and have a new record, "Fists Buried In Pockets"

title: Stacked and Shifted
description: Rivaling Eric's pink couch status is Andy, who as of this video, has appeared in 3 pink couches. You can download Get Bent's demo under the ALBUMS section for free, and also listen to Andy's Red and Blue AND State Lottery sessions. I love me some Get Bent.

title: Out-takes
description: The 31 Movies DVD is 99% done, and almost ready for your consumption. In the meantime, check out these hilarious out-takes and if you haven't seen the originals, go back in the archives and give them a watch.

title: Parking Lot
description: 3 great dudes from Lansing, Michigan. You can get their record for free on Quote Unquote records (donate!) or get it on CD or Vinyl from Bermuda Hawk or Los Diapers respectively. For fans of Smoking Popes and Dinosaur Jr. They also seem to love plaid and leaving without saying goodbye.

title: Self Check Out
description: From Silent Barn in Ridgewood Queens. It was pretty dark in the room so I lowered the frame rate, so that explains why it stutter. Expect a whole mess of videos from the road and continuous updates from here on out.

title: Attraction
description: Toby in his own backyard in Western Springs, Ill. This is a new song, which you can hear recorded on his myspace. Also check out his band Arkansas?, who is going on tour soon.

title: City
description: GB at the Boneyard in Brooklyn with new drummer Gerge. Hopefully this band will be heading out on tour, because I'm sure a lot of people are excited to see them. You can download their album in the downloads section.

title: Life On A Map
description: Patrick at the Foster's House in Western Springs. I love filming outside, most shows look the same and are really dark. It's nice to have a change of scenery every now and again. Also check out Toby's song that I posted early this week. This reminds me of a children's tv show.

title: Guitarmageddon
description: Virgins and Monikers came by last month to record a couple songs. It's taken me a while but I'm finally getting them up on the site. After recording the room dissolved into madness, with everyone picking up guitars and making noise.

title: Fingers
description: It was great hanging out/playing with buddies for a couple days on our respective tours. Being away from home and being able to see Sheena made me feel a lot better. Unfortunately I missed their Fest show, which seemed crazy, so instead enjoy some footage from their pre-fest Tallahassee show.

title: Purple Flowers
description: Sara came over a while back with Spoonboy, and I've taken forever to put this up. I was recently in Olympia, and just happened to be staying in the house that she lived. This worked out great considering I had no idea what the name of this song is. I'm sure she'll be on tour again soon, so make sure to check her out.

title: The Roof and the Rod
description: Tin Armor at this year's Fest. The cops came right before they were going to play. Luckily this pushed them back in time for everyone to run over and swarm this amazing band.

title: sometimes=all the time (duh).
description: Another recording from the Foster's. This time from Victor, who also plays drums in the band Arkansas? with Tobey. His voice/style reminds me of Red Animal War, for some reason. Either way, you should check him out. He is also pretty nasty at the drums.

title: Wave
description: Carly and Tom called everyone up on stage for some slow jams, which unfortunately ended with them ushering everyone back off. They are trying to win a contest over at Epitaph, so head over to the demo section and vote for them!

title: A Song In Memory Of Stormy The Rabbit
description: From the last day of the Asian Man tour. Sean and Ben plus members of Bomb the Music Industry and Sheena from Lemuria. This tour featured some of the nicest people playing music right now. I'm sure they all had a great time hanging out too, just listen to those solos!

title: i never get winded
description: This year's Fest was great because one venue, the Kickstand, was all buddy bands, plus is was all ages. Iron Chic played the first day, and the room was packed. It was weird seeing some of the same shows all the way in Florida, but great to see how much everyone loved them. This new song is amazing..

title: 1001 Open Hands
description: Good Luck at a weird show in Bloomington. I started moving my videos hosting over to Vimeo, so now you can embed them to your hearts desire. They are also going to be a lot higher resolution. No more stealing and putting them on YouTube. Enjoy!

title: Hobo Chili
description: Aaron came over a while back to record. It's been a while since he recorded, and he's been busy with a new A!A! record and a De La Hoya discography. Look for it all that stuff on Red Leader records, home of manatee stories.

title: I Knew This Day Would Come
description: At the Chop Chop gallery in Columbus, Ohio. They played a set comprised of all new songs, which was pretty amazing itself. This space is the home of Shout Out Loud printing, which does awesome screenprinting. Check them out in the link section.

title: Coney Island
description: I originally saw this band when they were called Castles about a year ago, and they blew me away with how tight they were. If you like this you should get their record "Only Way to Be Alone", which has a song with Dave Davison of Maps and Atlases on it.

title: Gasoline
description: This is from 2008's Fest in Gainesville, Florida. I had a bunch of bands come into a space we were using (Thanks Evan and Katie!) down the street from registration. So look forward to a bunch more in the near future. This song is brand new and was just released on the 1st, you can get it at chunksaah.com

title: blackbox.
description:
Nana Grizol, with members Theo, Madeline and Kate at the Hangar, in Athens, Georgia. This was the first night I decided to record "pink" couch tour footage outside of Fest. I think this came out amazing and sounds great.

Look for Madeline's recording coming soon too, and buy their record from Orange Twin.

title: You Ever Get Punched In the Face for Talking Too Much?
description:
Introducing...three guys, two guitars and an egg shaker. This was one of my favorite recordings from the Fest series. I loved their full length, and am looking forward to hearing their new record "Gasp Asp", which you can buy from Tiny Engines (http://www.tinyengines.net) or digitally from Lujo (http://www.lujorecords.com).

Also look forward to some new intros on the videos, courtesy of Jamtron!

title: Biggie Vs Super Bobby
description:
Fresh from the IYMI vault, we have one of the earliest recordings of Super Bobby, going up against the legendary Biggie Smalls. Unfortunately Jeff didn't make the cut for "Notorious".

He also rarely even comes to Brooklyn now.

title: Chelsea Hotel No. 2
description:
From 2008's Fest in Gainesville where I was finally able to coax Paul into recording something for the site. Things get a little tense at the end, especially when he left to get some [ice cream](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/31-movies/paul-baribeau-goes-to-get-ice-cream/).

This song is originally by Leonard Cohen.

title: Bricks
description:
This is the last recording I did on tour, and one of my favorites. It was done in the amazing Chop Chop gallery in Columbus, Ohio, after a fun night of music and the gadabout film fest.

Check out AW's split with Andy Cook, another IYMI alumni.

title: Things They Say
description:
Evan helped set this up at 2008's Fest in Gainesville. They did an awesome job the first time, but my camera decided to zoom in all the way right at the end. Luckily they were able to bang another great take in seconds.

They are recording now.

title: We'd Never Agree
description:
I waited all night before asking them about recording, luckily my good friend Kate took charge and we figured it all out. Bagels were produced, eaten and I knocked over multiple things.

They have a new record coming out, which sounds amazing, and has this song on it.

P.S. Eliot also contains members of The Ackleys.

title: Telephone Wires
description:
Awesome brother-sister duo from Santa Barbara, CA. We stayed with Rebecca recently and were treated to some amazing vegan pizzas and soup. We had a nice fire roaring when we recorded and a fun group surrounding them.

I think this is the first baritone ukelele and harp on IYMI. 2009 is nuts!

title: Go West
description:
I first saw Austin back in Bloomington, and was blown away by the amount of emotion in his songs. Luckily I was able to film him then and convince him to stop by during this year's Fest.

Check out his recent full length "Somebody Loves You" on Suburban Home, you won't regret it.

title: Unspoken Words
description:
So this is probably the farthest from pink couch setup I've gone. If anyone was going to do it, I'm glad it was these guys. MA are one of the most fun bands to watch, and some of the nicest people I know.

Ben also does some awesome print work which you can see on [flickr.](http://flickr.com/photos/bensears)

Album downloads from MA, and other side projects coming tonight!

title: Heartless
description:
From this past year's Fest in Gainesville. Chris was the first person to stop by our little setup, so he had to be the guinea pig for the whole operation.

This is an extra song off of their new record "It's Great To Be Alive", which is out on the 17th. They are on tour with Murder by Death now.

title: Hunger Strike
description: From the Hobo House, a place which hasn't had many shows in recent memory. This was overturned for Jared's birthday, when Nude Beach, Thousandaires, and Iron Chic played. This song was originally by the "super-group" Temple of the Dog. Lou and Mikey also filled in on guitar and vocals respectively.

title: We Bring the Booze
description:
I've known Trevor (the drummer), for a long time, so I was happy to bring his new band over to record, who were all awesome funny guys.

They recently played some shows with Bouncing Souls and Gaslight Anthem. Check them out on their upcoming March/April tour.

title: I Waited All Day
description:
I've been trying to get Madeline over to do a pink couch for over a year. I was finally able to work it out when we rolled through Athens on Halo Fauna tour and set up in the Hangar space.

I love the lighting in this video. Hopefully my new lighting setup will bring new life to the pink couch.

title: Small Snails
description:
This show was so much fun, with Little Lungs and PS Eliot also playing. I recorded Little Lungs at this show too, but it ended up not coming through the mic.

Check out Cheeky's Art of the Underground Single series contribution.

title: Begging to Bleed
description:
Rob came down and knocked out two different pink couches, between this and Ann Beretta at last year's Fest.

Foundation recently joined the Paper + Plastick crew, and will be releasing a full length on March 24th, both digitally and on vinyl.

title: Holy Hell
description:
I played with Marissa's other band Screaming Females in Charleston, SC on election day. We all had a lot of fun, and it was nice to see tri-state area buddies when your on the road. Especially when it randomly works out.

Noun is Marissa's solo project, and it's amazing. Check out her myspace to hear more, and go see her slay at guitar in Screaming Females.

title: Pigeon
description:
These Chicago boys rolled into the city on Monday to play a snow laden New York. I couldn't drag myself into Manhattan, but I made it to their Southpaw show the next day. I recorded 90% of their set, so I'll put it up here soon.

We also discussed a future pink couch for their new record, and one for Dave's Cast Spells project.

title: Nightmare Sisters
description:
Like Michael Keaton in Multiplicity, Jon cloned himself 3 times and made them all play in different bands for him. Luckily his singing is a million times better the MK's acting or my cat's meowing (which he requested).

Check out his other projects including the Color Wheels on that myspace thing that everyone uses.

title: Triggers
description:
This is the first time I've seen them with their new drummer Matt. So, I got to hear a bunch of new songs that they've been working on since then.

Mike Campbell screaming has to be one of the most jarring visuals in my life. I was telling him how this band is what I envision playing on the other side of a portal to hell.

Get their EP in the albums section for free.

title: Fuck the Border
description:
From their Brooklyn show with Bridge and Tunnel / Paint it Black. Dan Yemin actually came out and sang for this, which is one of my favorites off Today's Empires, Tomorrow's Ashes.

Hopefully they are coming to town near you.

title: You Always Wanted A Song, Right?
description:
I'm supposed to tell a bunch of people in Gainesville that I'm putting this up. Well here it is, someone tell Katie and Evan. You know them right? Pepsi?

CP is equal parts Less than Jake and Rehasher, and you can get their newest record on Paper + Plastick.

title: Atlantic City
description:
Part of their Darkness on the Edge of Your Town tour in which they play a bunch of awesome Springsteen songs. When you pair these together you get this amazing chemistry of charm and fun. They've got a couple of days left, make sure you check it out.

Ginger also plays in Good Luck, while Paul stars in his own one man show.

title: Damned Distractions
description:
This was taken in a crowded Athens restaurant, where a couple of weird customers wouldn't budge during the filming. I approached Patrick a bunch of times about recording, but it never worked out. Luckily I have some very persuasive friends ( thanks Ginger and Jeff!), who made it happen. Also thank you Cara Beth and Tatiana for supplying the sweet digs.

If you can name all the people in the video, you get a star.

title: In the Studio
description:
Katie and I recently visited Cheeky while they were recording at Chris Pierce's Technical Ecstasy studios in New Jersey. We had a lot of fun hanging out with them and the Don Giovanni crew, who are peppered throughout this clip.

They have a full length LP called "What The Heck", which is going to be released on Don Giovanni in the near future.

title: Cold Chillin' Cold Chillin'
description:
Jeff likes to refer to this as "The Show of Mistakes", but I thought it was a lot fun, despite the weird atmosphere and ridiculous cover.

This is the first song off of their new album "Scrambles" which you can download for free from quoteunquote records or buy from Asian Man on vinyl.

title: Chase and Sanborn (A Quale of Friendship)
description:
Mike and Matt came by during Fest and looked honestly scared while they were playing. The two goons on the left and right were eyeballing them the whole time, threatening to break their kneecaps. I think it had something to do with their gambling debts.

They must have spent all their money recording their new record "Strange Spring Air", which you can get for free at Quote Unquote. ( [quoteunquoterecords.com/qur024.zip](http://www.quoteunquoterecords.com/qur024.zip) )

title: Exchange
description:
Short film about making friends. This is going to be part of new series that Eric is working on with his Bloomington crew. This video features music and acting by Good Luck's Matt Tobey, who eerily looks like Dom in the beginning of this video.

Is this a trend for Eric? Watch out Mike Dumps, you're next on the list for video partner. Also look forward to more videos from this crew. I'm glad Eric is finally back in the ring.

title: Every Soldier
description:
Stinking it up at the Charleston. Unfortunately the bass drum was sliding all over the place, so this is the only one that'll work out of the set. Thanks to Katie for filming the show and Matt for letting me show up that late.

Thousandaires / Little Lungs split coming soon on some weirdo label. You can get our free three song demo under the albums section.

title: Get Up + Get Out
description:
I was able to hang out with the GGA lads while they were on tour with good buddies Lemuria. They were all crammed into one van, and I'm surprised the 6 of them made it out alive. I'm a big fan of Russ' guitar playing in this band, especially his strumming style.

Check out some of their releases at Art of the Underground. This song is from a 7inch called Fifty Hours on AARBR Records.

title: Polish Hill
description:
Yoni sent me an email a while back, and I finally was able to work out a time for him to swing by.

Unfortunately I never gave him my number or told him about my lack of a doorbell. So he stood outside for about 20 minutes, while I sat here wondering where he was. Luckily we were able to work it out, with a Facebook message.

This is a little different than his full band, Yoni Gordon and the Goods, so check that out if you want a more electrified view.

title: Who Was That Man
description:
I've always been a big fan of this Athen's trio, ever since I heard them 3 years ago. This video is from last year's Fest in Gainesville at the Kickstand. HNM were one of the many amazing bands that played this venue, which made it harder and harder for me to leave.

This is from "It's Called Leaning Back" which you can buy from Salinas Records.

title: Shout Out
description:
This video is from part of my marathon stint of filming at last year's Berea Fest. The whole thing was only two days, but I'm surprised at how much energy everyone had after being cooped up in the same room for so long. You can see a bunch of smiling faces, including Ginger, who seems to enjoying a nice apple.

Totally Michael has a new self-title record out on IHEARTCOMIX records

title: Right Away
description: This is from the same show as the newest Maps and Atlases video. PIM has some of most dynamic drumming I've seen in a while. He plays so effortlessly and smooth, it's hard to wrap my mind around it. This version of the song has an extended outro, that gets out of hand with all of the out of time clapping.

title: Shoplifter
description:
From Columbus, Ohio, comes two awesome individuals named Ryan and Lisa. We've been talking about doing a LTTM Pink Couch for a while, but it's been hard getting them this way over the last two years. They recently did a tour with local buddies, Saint Seneca, who also recorded that day. So look forward to that!

You might also know Ryan from the threesome Delay, who are also from Columbus. They also have a big hand in running the amazing Bereafest each year, which provides 1/4 of the content on this site.

title: Mouthbreather
description:
Looking back over the video on IYMI, I noticed that there hasn't been one of Laura playing live. Seeking to correct that mistake, I brought my camera to her last show, where she played with her full Cans lineup ( minus 1 ). This song is from their upcoming 7inch on Mandible Records.

Recently it was Laura's birthday, so make sure to wish her a happy birthday in the comments.

title: Lives Like Mine
description:
Mike and In The Red was supposed to drop by a couple of months ago, unfortunately they came down with something on their way through New Jersey. In a karmic turnaround, Mike and tour buddies Ninja Gun were able to meet and see Willie Nelson play on their way through the Garden State. You can even see a signature on the guitar if you look close enough.

Mike has recently decided to devote his life to just traveling and playing his music. You can read about it over at buddy site Punknews.org, and get his new album for free at liveslikemine.suburbanhomerecords.com.

title: Stars Were Exploding
description:
So you probably can tell that I love this band a lot. So I can ensure that there will be no end to the amount of videos of them on this site. This video is from their recent show at Death By Audio in Brooklyn.

Also, I've rearranged the names of the videos, so the other version of this song is called "Bringing them back to life"

While they were here they also recorded a video for the new Blue Couch Sessions.

title: Horror
description:
This is from last year's Make Music Day in Brooklyn. I love being able to shoot outdoors, it always looks so amazing with all the colors and scenery. On a side note, make sure to buy their newish 7 inch "Hot Green", it's amazing.

Everyone in Brooklyn should come see this band tonight. Check out the events link at the top of site to find out more.

title: True Blue Rocker
description:
We've been working on getting the whole Used Kids crew over here, but with their multiple bands, it's been anything but easy. In the end, Nato decided that it probably be easier just to come over and do it himself.

They have a new full length "Ready, Aim, Fire." coming out on Salinas Records. This song is even newer than that.

title: Collective Gasp
description:
Lou Fontana was recently added to this amazing three piece, and this was one of his first shows with them. Lou plays/ed Small Arms Dealer, On The Might of Princes, and his own project God's Gift To Women. A Pink Couch from GGTW will be up later this week.

This is probably the fastest I've ever gotten a video up.

title: Front Yard Screamers (Kitchen Kissers)
description:
Coody and his happy bunch rolled in here with Mike Hale a little while back. They had just come from New Jersey, where they had randomly run into Willie Nelson, while wandering around New Brunswick.

This is off of their Suburban Home release, "Restless Rubes".

title: Israeli Caves
description:
Despite my current money situation, I'm going to try and get the finished videos uploaded and off my computer.

This is from their show at the Southpaw with Pattern is Movement. They will also be filming a blue couch session in the future.

title: Stacked and Shifted
description:
I wanted to put up a good version of Face Mush, unfortunately the vocals didn't come out in that big room. This is a reminder for people to check out their new song "Things Not Done Changed" on their myspace. Although this band is now defunct, they have a 3 song EP coming out soon, which may or may not be on this site. I can't remember.

Check out Mike Dump's face at (00:53).

title: Fado Song
description:
Lou and Maureen stopped by a little while ago to bring two additions to the pink couch. This is the first Flamenco guitar and flute ever to grace this apartment. The song is off of their album "Corrida".

Check out Lou's other projects, including Small Arms Dealer, On the Might of Princes, and now Fellow Project.

title: Get Rich or Die Trying Part 2
description:
Eric came by after a night of playing at the magical Lost & Found/Lulus/Greenpoint Gator. I think some bagels were involved, although I might be mixing that up with every other day.

OP!!! put out a lot of records and play tons of shows, so definitely check them out.

title: Margie
description:
This is from this year's Crucial Fun Fest. You'll be seeing a lot of Eric in these videos, since he was always in the front of the pack at each show. Thanks to Anthony for putting together an amazing weekend.

Heathers are currently on tour with Ghost Mice. This is off of their recent full length -Here, Not There-. They will also be recording a pink couch when they come through.

title: (Mr.) Spaceman
description:
Mainly consisting of the vastly talented Rogers' brothers, BH has been infusing Bloomington, Indiana with some crazy energy lately. They recently reassembled a new group of players, which include a some amazing sting players. Addison's drums also consist of a suitcase, foot pedal and snare drum.

You can download their full length "Old Friends" in the album section.

title: Beloit, Wisconsin
description:
From this year's Crucial Fun Fest in Lexington, KY. Arkansas? is a 3 great dudes from the Chicago area. They all also have solo projects that you can check out, which include Victor V-B, Drum and an Open Window and the self-titled Toby Foster.

Arkansas? has 11 song cd which you can get on their myspace.

title: No Names
description:
These four lovely people came over during their recent tour with Letters to the Moon. At first the garbage can was set up on the floor, and it was making the camera shake like crazy. It got really distracting and felt like you are witnessing an earthquake.

You used to be able to download their demo from ohym.org, but the site seems to be in the works. I'll link to it once it goes back up.

title: The Body is A Temple/Sunshine On Your Potholes
description:
I hadn't heard of this Chicago 3-piece until the day they came to our fair city. I'm really glad I was able to make it out to one of their shows, because they were amazing live.

You can buy their new record "Visibility Zero" from Residue Records or off of Vinyl Collective. For fans of Leatherface.

title: Waiter
description:
Ellie and Louise are back in the states again touring with good buddies Ghost Mice. If you haven't seen them yet, make sure you take the time to catch one of their last shows on this tour before they head home.

This is a brand new song that they just recently named.

title: Brooklyn Pool
description:
This is by no means our best show, but I figure it's good enough to put up. We hit a lot of weird notes, I stop early, and Ghost Mice just played so everyone had already left. We're working on recording a 3 song seven inch. We never practice, and it makes us sad. We are all turning into our fathers.

Lando took the helm on recording this. Thanks Lando!

title: Gray Like the Whale
description:
Ryan managed to play every night at Crucial Fun Fest this year. He played bass w/ the Sidekicks on Friday, guitar w/ Delay|LTTM on Saturday, and guitar w/ Lose the 'Tude on Sunday. Suffice to say, he got a lot free drinks and black bean burgers. Austin also played with Lose the 'Tude. Jesse went hungry all weekend.

This show was a lot of fun, and it's always great to see these three play. Kids also love to run around in circles for them.

title: Fruit Tea
description:
I first checked out this band the night before they played with Slingshot Dakota at Death By Audio. I liked what I heard, so I quickly emailed them before I went the show. Unfortunately I had to run, and didn't get a chance to talk to them.

Luckily, some mutual friends stepped in, and Cody was able to get a hold of me. Check them out while they are on tour and make sure to pick up their new record "Microscopic Metronomes" if you like it. They also recorded a session with Daytrotter that should be out soon.

title: Hop On a Sea Cow and Manatee Up
description:
For some crazy reason I hadn't really listened to this band until recently. But with the addition of Matt #2 (American War), it is really hard not to stop and pay attention.

Now that sounded professional. They are recording right this second, but until that record comes out, make sure to pick up their recent release "Sam" on Whoa Oh.

title: Excercise for the Public
description:
In this episode Roger Valencia talks about exercising.

Brought to you by Jeff from Jamtron.com and Super Bobby. Check out his vimeo page for some of his more recent videos, including some amazing green screen technology.

title: Only Babies Cry
description:
Happy Fourth of July!

I was going to put up another Springsteen cover song from Paul and Ginger, but I couldn't find my footage in time. So as a last resort, I'm posting this footage from Crucial Fun Fest, where Paul randomly showed up and played a set.

Paul is soon to join Eric in the IYMI-retiree program.

title: See Ya In The Funny Papers
description:
On a rainy day in June, a bunch of people packed in the Naideau garage in Northport, LI for awesome music and some food. Unfortunately it rained all night, and bugs were in full force.

This is from one of the 3 shows HDWJTH? has played in it's lifetime. You can get their 5 song demo free in the ALBUMS section.

title: Tales Of Success
description:
For episode nine of Cob Job, we decided that we would each make a movie without the help of any other member of cob job. The theme was selected: "Life in the Fast Lane." Five funny shorts came out of the project.

This is Eric Ayotte's fast lane short movie.

title: I (Won't) Give Up
description:
The lead member of New Jersey's Communipaw strikes out on his lonesome.

He's already released a couple of awesome albums, some of which you can download for free. Make sure to check out his new record "Fire & Gold" and Communipaw's new self-titled.

Thanks to Brian for braving faulty equipment, and lousy city traffic.

title: Between Resistance & Community: The Long Island Do-it-yourself Punk Scene
description:
This original documentary focuses on a group of kids who have built a community around a love of music and a passion for creating an alternative to dominant consumerist society. By putting on shows in basements, booking and traveling on national tours, and releasing their own records, these kids attempt to distance themselves from the corporate influence that they consider not only unnecessary, but unwelcome.

This fascinating look into the workings of a DIY punk community also examines the scene's shortcomings, such as undercurrents of sexism as well as the problem of self sufficiency, which pressures some to seek out the same corporate avenues to which the scene strives to be an alternative.

Features live footage and interviews with Latterman, On the Might of Princes, The Insurgent, Seven Days of Samsara, plus show-goers, scenesters, zinesters, and activists.
Includes deleted scenes, extra live footage, and follow up segments on Latterman, scene members, and women in punk.

You can buy a copy for $13 at Microcosm Publishing : http://www.microcosmpublishing.com/catalog/videos/426/

title: There's Never A Reason Not to Party
description:
'In name of DIY', here's an old video from 2001 of Long Island's Latterman. This is the first incarnation of the band with drummer Bryce Hackford and Dan Sposato on guitar. This band has generated a lot of lesser than (<) , greater than (>) sign talk with their hydra like ability to form great bands.

This video is from the Walklor Productions' Between Resistance & Community: The Long Island Do-it-yourself Punk Scene. You can watch the trailer in the Series section.

Check out the audience to see some babies.

title: Berea Fest IV Trailer
description:
Tomorrow is the first day of this year's Berea Fest. I look forward to this day every year, and I'm always sad when it's inevitably over. If you aren't coming, and you are in the area, make sure to head on over.

Music starts at 6:30PM on Friday July 17th. Check Bereafest.org for more information.

title: Kentucky Gentlemen
description:
This is an unreleased track off their new Red Scare record 'Hold On Dodge', which you can listen to here, or buy off iTunes. Check out them on their current tour with the Sidekicks and Cobra Skulls, and their upcoming interview on Generic Insight Radio.

This is going to be my last Pink Couch until I get back from Vermont in last August. Luckily for me, we were able to squeeze these two in before I left.

title: Old Men
description:
I've seen 2HF (THF) play a handful of times, and I've always been impressed with them. Unfortunately my camera hasn't, so a lot of the footage came out unusable. This is off their EP Rivers, which you can download for free from dontgiveupohio.com

Luckily I was able to capture a song or two from this year's Berea Fest. Expect more videos to come.

title: I Burned the Crops
description:
I tried to take a backseat for most of Berea Fest, and only film from the back. Fortunately I love this band too much to stand all the way in the back on a chair.

Check them out on their current tour with Cobra Skulls and The Menzingers. This is also off their recent Whoa Oh Records release "Sam".

title: I Thought You Were (But You're Not)
description: Another video from this year's Berea Fest in Berea, Ohio. Sorry about the crazy camera movements, I didn't have my tripod and I had to hold the camera over my head. This is off their 5 Song EP "Have You Heard?"

title: Primary Youth
description:
It seems like I'm getting worse at recording as each day goes by. In this video my wrist is shaking like a wet dog, and the zooms are crazy fast and sickening. Sorry to anyone who tries to watch this.

This is a new song from Columbus' Tin Armor, from this year's Bereafest.

title: Grow Fins, Turkey
description:
I stumbled upon this footage recently, and I was surprised that I had never put it up.

This is from 2008's Fest in Gainesville. I had so much fun at the Kickstand last year, and I hope that it's the same way this year. I miss Cheeky.

title: I'm Not Dead
description:
Their set was pretty hilarious, with each member messing with one another. Most of it was based on the drummer working early the next morning, forcing them to leave the show early.

H&A reminded me of Fugazi, although I'm sure a million bands get that comparison.

This is from a recent Thousandaires show in Buffalo, NY.

title: Too Much
description:
I filmed this video almost a year ago when they were on tour with Virgins. Since then Ryan has moved to Brooklyn, and started Stolen Parts with a few notable dudes around here.

Annoyingly, I kept forgetting the name of the song each time he told me, and it also got lost in the bowels of the Dailymotion vault.

This is from their split with Ohio's Delay.

title: Tuesday Blues
description:
Championed by Ginger Alford, SW have been causing quite a stir in their home of Bloomington, Indiana. Halo Fauna had the chance to play with them this last Spring/Summer, and I thought they were pretty awesome.

They are doing a short tour with Good Luck soon, so make sure to check them out.

title: I Wait
description:
I got to Bereafest this year a little late, but luckily I was able to catch the end of Steve and Pat's set. Steve is rocking an amazing white headset in the video, which fits perfectly.

You can get their side of their newest split in the Albums section. Steve plays in The Sidekicks.

title: Dance
description:
Cob Job Promotional Choreography:
One balmy day Lewis insinuated that David should demonstrate his latest steps...

This is just a promo for their full length show, which can be seen at http://www.vimeo.com/cobjob. Check it out!

title: Silver Spade
description:
My good buddy Isaac from Buffalo, NY recently clued me into this great three piece. I shot them a message a couple of weeks ago and we worked it all out.

They are playing a free show at SUNY Purchase tonight, so if you are in the area check them out.

On a side note, the Pink Couch Sessions are back after a unintentional summer recess. Stay tuned for some cool new bands.

title: Second Name
description:
Frances has this crazy habit of letting me know her shows hours before she gets into the city. Luckily I was able to 'hop' over there and catch their round robin set.

This is from their 10in on Salinas Records called Wretches. You can get it here.
http://www.salinasrecords.com/release/hop-along/wretches/

title: Another Bag of Bones
description:
Kevin of Big Scary Monsters Records gave me the contact for this Kevin, who I've been following since his days in the Miracle of '86.

Despite some BQE traffic, we were able to squeeze him onto the big stinky couch. I'm really glad we did.

This song is off of his recent 7 inch on Academy Fight Song. You can buy it here.
http://www.academyfightsong.com/releases.html

title: Fireettoowweerr
description:
The Max Levine Ensemble performs "Fireettoowweerr" at the Insubordination Fest Pre-Show, June 25th, 2009. The sound clips are from a radio broadcast where Ben Weasel (of Screeching Weasel) announced his hatred for the Max Levine Ensemble.

Text of the clip: "My worst nightmare is to find somebody that likes that band and also likes my stuff, because then immediately, IMMEDIATELY, I'm going to start questioning myself and asking myself what am I doing wrong??"

The full radio broadcast can be downloaded here: http://a1135.g.akamai.net/f/1135/18227/1h/cchannel.download.akamai.com/18227/podcast/MADISON-WI/WTSO-AM/Weasel%20Radio%207-23-08.mp3

title: Untitled
description:
This is from their recent show with Maps and Atlases. I can assure you that the pieces of paper taped to the stands are only there for a cover song they did later in the set.

Someone told Laura that she shouldn't count into songs that she starts. I think it was Beethoven.

title: Follow Me
description:
Despite living in the U.K. , I've had a bunch of random run-ins with Dave over the last two years. He plays in the band the Steal, who are one of the first bands on IYMI. I also had to contact him this summer about printing the Lemuria shirt that I did for their recent overseas tour.

Now he's sitting on my couch, walking around my neighborhood, playing shows with my friends, and it's really awesome. Check him out for the limited time that he is here.

This is from his album Intersections.
You can buy it from Runner Up(US) http://www.runneruprecords.com or Banquet(UK) http://www.banquetrecords.com.

title: Plainclothes
description:
Weirdly enough, one of the members of this band lives right down the street for me. Unfortunately this didn't help him get his sweater back, which I've been holding hostage for the last week.

This song is unreleased, but you can pick up their record "Why There Are Mountains".

P.S. I found the sweater.

title: Unwritten
description:
Lauren and I have been trying to work out a Pink Couch for over the last year, but it's always been crazy trying to bring together all of their members. Luckily I was able to catch them before a show in Brooklyn, and we sat down got it done.

Check them out w/ the Gaslight Anthem and at this year's Fest.

title: Lake Erie Nosedive
description:
Another band that I've been wanting to have over since the inception of the site. Luckily I was able to work something out when they came through on their tour down to Fest with Lansing's Cheap Girls.

They have a new record on Paper + Plastick, called "In What Way" that is amazing. Check it out.

title: Dialtones
description:
At Fest this year, I had a 0% chance of seeing my friends play at Common Grounds. So instead of standing in a never ending line all day, I hopped on over to the Market Street Pub, and caught one of Boston's Ringers final shows.

It was awesome, despite some random dudes acting like idiots. This song is from their split with Ampere.

title: Strange Behavior
description:
I've had been talking to Jon over the internet for about a month, when he told me that he'd be in the city for one of the last Nakatomi shows. I ran into him at the tail end of the show, but unfortunately I had forgotten that he'd be there, so I stared at him blankly as he talked about setting up this pink couch.

These three are from Philadelphia, so they are back in New York all the time.

title: Evening Constitutional
description:
From this year's Fest at the Kickstand. They recently released "Hello" on Quote Unquote, and it's a really amazing record. Get it for free and throw some buckaroonies to the guys.

The band features members of Shinobu, Pteradon and Classics of Love.

http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/blog/recommendations/hard-girls/

title: Oklahoma
description:
This was one of my most forgetful days of the year. I literally had to run back and forth to my car 3 times to get stuff that I had left in the back of my van. Luckily everyone was able to amuse themselves with Toby.

Coming over from Manchester, TAM has been in the city for the last 3 weeks, playing a bunch of shows starting at CMJ and up until their departure yesterday. Hopefully they had a good time.

Listen to their latest album "Another City, Another Sorry" here.

theansweringmachine.bandcamp.com/
I recommend "Cliffer".

title: Lift Me Up
description:
Hopefully sometime in the future we can work out a Pink Couch, but until then I've got some footage from his last show which happened right down the street at Dom's house.

Jason is amazing at inciting sing-a-longs, and he has a faithful group of followers who know every single word. I don't think this is on any records, but please correct me if I am wrong.

title: Atlantic Bridge
description:
Despite my love for the band, I have never had anything of Little Lungs to put up on the site over the last 3 years. Dodgy PAs and dark apartments have forced IYMI to go Lungless for far too long. Luckily ( I say that a lot), Angie and Josh live right across the street, so we were able to quickly remedy the situation.

Pick up their 7inch from Salinas Records, it was one of favorites last year.
http://www.salinasrecords.com/release/little-lungs/hoist-me-up/

title: Baby Jesus
description:
One of the first times I heard this band was when we played together in Charleston, SC on Election Day. Since then they have played some crazy shows, opening for Dinosaur Jr, and their recent tour with Jack White's new band Dead Weather.

This is from a recent show at Asbury Lanes, a benefit for 180NJ. Marissa also stopped by early this year to record a pink couch for her solo project Noun.

title: Now is Better than Before
description:
I first heard about Tallahassee's Holiday Shores from their session with Greenpoint's Chocolate Bobka. After viewing the high production values of that video, it only made sense to have them sit on my droopy, lumpy couch.

Unfortunately we didn't have enough guitars or percussion instruments for everyone, so we settled on cover originally by Jonathan Richman of the Modern Lovers.

title: Dead Sailors
description:
Franz is a busy man. Between playing in The Hold Steady, World/Inferno Friendship society, Anti-Social Music and on a million different records, he has found the time to release a solo album.

He came over here and blasted out 3 songs in a row. It was actually pretty amazing how quickly we were done. Now what do I do with the other two...

You can buy "St. Sebastian Of The Short Stage" from Team Science Records
http://bit.ly/jEBKX

title: The Vapors
description:
Despite an awful trip down to the city, we had a pretty fun time recording this. At first I was really skeptical about the cans, but I think it came out incredibly unique sounding, with just a touch of crazy.

Paul's Grandfather hails from Fredonia, NY and they have a new EP out that you can buy from [CD Baby](http://www.cdbaby.com/cd/paulsgrandfather).

They've got the muppet bounce down pat.

title: Just Another Day
description:
Pulling double duty as Cast Spells' backing band, they were still able to pull off a pitch perfect performance. I'm usually not a fan of the singing drummer, but I keep adding exceptions to that rule everyday.

This is off of their recent full length "Only Way to be Alone".

title: A Badge
description:
From Maxwell's basement in Hoboken, NJ. We assembled Dave Davison, Good Old War and Hezikiah Jones to create an amazing backing band for Cast Spells.

Sorry about any shaking, it was really cramped in there, and I had a hard time getting everything.

title: All Star Baseball 2000
description:
Part of the Instant Gratification Monthly Short Movie Challenge held by the Cob Job crew of Bloomington. This entry was for the theme "Since You've Been Gone", and features the talent of Eric Ayotte and Paul Baribeau.

Visit their page at Vimeo to watch more videos from the series, or join in on the fun by submitting your own.

title: Layers
description:
Coming down from New Paltz way, Klessa features some of the best people that the little town has to offer. The band manages to combine many different instruments into an amazing full sounding package.

They put on an amazing live show that you should definitely check out. Klessa recently released "Aloha Demons"

title: A Healthy Time
description:
In celebration of the new year, I'm posting a pink couch from my favorite band of '09. Over the last year, I fell in love with the Sam EP and overplayed Weight of Air to the point of exhaustion. They've made a lot of Top 10 lists this year, and for a good reason.
