Check back later today for some live footage from their show at Death By Audio.

title: Static Mouth
description:
This is from the holiday show at Death By Audio that Jeff from Bomb the Music Industry! hosted. I was really excited to see them play this, considering that I had missed them a couple of times throughout the year.

This is the last thing I'll post for the rest of the year. Happy New Year's everyone. Buy "Weight of Air".

title: Livingston
description:
This was from a great show at Philadelphia's Terrordome. I had recently heard of FSTT from some random internet sleuthing, and was excited to see that they were playing our show. It's sounds like a combination of Braid and the staccato guitar tone of Kickball.

Download their EP here, [For Serious This Time - When You're In It](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/for-serious-this-time/when-you-re-in-it)

title: Get Outta Here
description:
From their last show at Death By Audio in Williamsburg. I have a bunch more footage, but I thought this one looked really great, and showed all the people who loved this band during their brief stint together.

Watch Brian hit the high note.

title: Pray for Tucson
description:
At the very last minute I realized that The Loved Ones' Dave Hause would be coming through the city for a couple of shows with Brian Fallon of the Gaslight Anthem. I quickly shot an email over to the invaluable Vanessa at Fat Wreck (thanks to Bryne for reminding me). Within two days of the show we were able to figure out a window of time that Dave could come over.

Dave is currently recording a solo album before he returns his attention back to the Loved Ones record. He's also going on a short tour in February through Canada and Midwest US. The Loved Ones will also appear at this year's [Harvest of Hope](http://harvestofhopefest.com/).

This is from a show at 538 Johnson. You can't really hear the vocals, but at least we were able to shoot from above. This is from our demo which you can download from the Free Albums section. We have a Fellow Project split coming out soon on Kiss of Death Records.

Slingshot Dakota, Captain We're Sinking, Nude Beach, Spraynard and Sundials also played.

title: Past the Barbary
description:
Ava Luna was first brought up in my interview with Laura Stevenson. Since then I've been trading emails back and forth with them about having them over for a Pink Couch.

This is the 1st recording to use a drum machine, and they also were able to fit 7 people on the couch, beating out Defiance, Ohio. The music flows together perfectly, it's amazing how little I had to do. Definitely check this band out.

You can download their latest EP from [Environmental Aesthetics](http://the.shop.environmentalaesthetics.com/album/ea007-ava-luna-services-ep) for free or donation.

title: First Down
description:
Lindsay and Mercy returned recently to play two Tin Kitchen reunion shows with good buddies P.S. Eliot. I've always wanted to have her over to record, but Houston isn't the closest city to New York.

If you like what you hear, you can also download [Football, etc](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/football-etc/first-down/) and [Tin Kitchen's](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/tin-kitchen/grace-ep/) records in the Album section.

title: Open Letter to the Scene
description:
Before going the solo route, Walter was part of some seminal New York bands over the last 20 years including Gorilla Biscuits, Youth of Today, Quicksand, Rival Schools, and Walking Concert.

He is currently working on a full length that is going to be split released by Academy Fight Song (US), Big Scary Monsters (UK), Dine Alone Records (Canada), Arctic Rodeo (Europe), and High Hopes Records (Japan). This is the title track off the upcoming record.

Thanks to BSM and RJ at AFS for setting this up.

title: Shrinking Coasts
description:
Thanks to the last minute recommendation of Joanne from Obsession Collection, I was able to have these 3 lovely people over to record. Vio/Miré is the solo project from Brendan Glasson, a Providence, Rhode Island native. He's joined on the couch by Liz Isenberg, who recently accompanied Deer Tick on tour as a guest vocalist.

They are currently on their way down to South by Southwest in Austin, Texas. Stay tuned for a solo pink couch from Liz Isenberg, which was recorded while they were here.

You can buy his album "March,2007" from [Leisure Class Records](http://alum.hampshire.edu/~bhs03/lcr_store.html).

title: Baby Bones
description:
This might be the first time we've had two siblings on seperate videos. A couple of months ago we had younger brother [Brian Bond](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/brian-bond/i-wont-give-up/) over.

PJ released a new full length "You Didn't Know I Was Alphabetical" on NJ's [Black Numbers](http://www.theblacknumbers.com/). He was also on tour with Billy Wallace, so stay tuned for Billy's pink couch recording.

Thanks to Chris C. and Mike S. for making the first 2 minutes of our interaction weird.

title: Seen the Light
description:
Billy sets out on his own after spending the last 6 years playing with his band The Wading Girl and as a member of Tim Barry's backing band.

He would fit perfectly on a label like Suburban Home, right next to Austin Lucas and Chad Price.

title: Hey, I'm Drowning!
description:
While checking out Liz's music online, I stumbled upon another video website, [One Night Music](http://www.onenightmusic.com/music/session/liz-isenberg/). All of the song that Liz recorded on the site were amazing, and I encourage you to check it out. ONM films in variety of cool places, which is great when you want to take a break from the big Pink blob.

You can also see Liz in my favorite Pink Couch with [Vio/Mire](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/vio-mire/shrinking-coasts/)

title: Stay Calm
description:
Kenny stopped by with RJ of [Academy Fight Song](http://academyfightsong.com/) a little while back after taking the train in from Philly. Originally in the band The Starting Line, Kenny has went off on his own and started Person L.

This is off his 2009 release "The Positives".

title: Hail Mary
description:
Thankfully, this Alabama four piece is able to play shows here every couple of months. Even with the small gaps in time, I wish they were a more permanent staple of the Tri-state area scene. They are one of my favorite live bands.

This off their [Salinas Records](http://www.salinasrecords.com/release/p-s-eliot/introverted-romance-in-our-troubled-minds/) full length "Introverted Romance in our Troubled Minds".

They also have a new 7" out on [Freedom School Records](http://www.freedomschoolrecords.com/)

title: Love Me a Good Microcosm
description:
Ex-My American Heart member, Brian Warren, stopped by for a quick session a couple of weeks ago. We were able to finish it in one take, and then proceeded to wait for the next 30 minutes for his van to get back from paying a NYC parking ticket.

Don't miss Weatherbox out during their May tour with Stegosaur and [Look Mexico](http://www.doghouserecords.com/). Check out their recent record "The Cosmic Drama" on [Doghouse Records](http://www.doghouserecords.com/).

title: Pale Moonlight
description:
This is from their show last month at the Knitting Factory with Bridge & Tunnel and Yesterday's Ring. They recently released a new full length, and coming back across the pond in May.

Watch out for Frankie's sick dance moves.

title: American Tour Part One
description:
Day 1 of the American War/Andy cook tour 2010. We left our comfy homes for about a week and a half to bring music to the peeps. Thanks to everyone who helped us on the way. There is more parts to come. This was the first time i ever used my new camera and sound equipment. So if anything sounds or looks weird, I'm sorry. (but not that sorry) - Jon Washington

Live songs in video:
[Cheap Girls](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/author/cheap-girls/) - Sleeping Weather
American War - Gardens and Smoke

Look for more videos from Jon Washington in the future, along with more updates from this tour. Don't forget to download American War's record [Rhetoric](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/american-war/rhetoric/) from the [Free Albums](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/category/albums) section.

title: Where Do We Go From Here
description:
Matt Pryor is here to kick off a new year for the site. His new record "May Day" just came out today, so make sure to grab it! The album was released under his own label, Nightshoes Syndicate, and on [Big Scary Monsters](http://www.bsmrocks.com/main.html) in the UK. Matt is currently on tour with [Into It Over It](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/into-it-over-it/ravenswood/), Chris Conley of Saves to Day and more, on the "Where's the Band?" tour.

You can hear the whole album on [Bandcamp](http://bsmrocks.bandcamp.com/album/may-day) and also buy it from [iTunes](http://itunes.apple.com/us/album/may-day-bonus-track-version/id487036459).

Thanks to Lisa, Bryan, Edan, Matt and Death By Audio for having us over! This session was on my "must-have" list when I started. It's nice to be able to cross one off finally!

title: American Tour Part Two
description:
Day 2 of the 2010 American War tour. Chicago, IL. More to come soon!

Songs include.
American War - Bricks
Andy Cook - Grow Strong

Grow strong is slightly cut down for time. Filmed by Jon Washington.

title: Ice Hotel
description:
Dinosaur Bones hail from Toronto and will be on tour at the end the month, traveling down the east coast. I had a lot of fun with these guys, and I'm excited that they will be back soon.

Over the last couple of months I've been dealing with a lot of broken equipment. The latest thing to go was my laptop, which stored all the PCS audio on it. At one point I was able to recover the files, but something happened. It's only for a couple of the sessions, but I'm going to re-watch them and decide whether they'll work without the external audio. Sorry for the delay in posting. I think I'm back!

title: American Tour Part Three
description:
Day 3 of the 2010 American War tour. Chicago, IL. With a special guest appearance. More to come soon!

Songs include.
American War-Old Love in Young Bodies
Sloppy Joe-My Girl, My World
http://www.myspace.com/sloppyjoeandth...
Filmed and everything'd by Jon Washington

title: Cut the Cord
description:
RVIVR perform Cut The Cord Live at The Grist Mill in Ontario, Canada. This is off of their recent self-titled full length on [Rumbletowne Records](http://www.rumbletowne.com/), which you can buy or download for free at their website. I saw them twice on their recent US tour, and they were amazing both times. Do yourself a favor and [download the record](http://www.rumbletowne.com/rtr/rtr-releases/RTR-009), it's so good.

This is the first in a series of videos from Ontario's The Grist Mill. Erik posts tons of videos of bands who come through. Stay tuned for more from awesome bands like O Pioneers!!! and Junior Battles.

title: Sore
description:
Another video that I was able to save from the wrecked hard drive of a computer I once had. ARMS was once the solo project of Harlem Shakes' Todd Goldstein. Over the last year, he has put together a full band and started releasing records as a three piece.

You can also [download their recent EP](http://www.armsarms.com/) for free from their website.

title: So Buttons
description: Taken from this year's Hillstock festival in Clinton Hill, Brooklyn. SW were one of the final bands to play that weekend, and unfortunately they were also the last straw for neighbors. They were forced to stop on their second to last song.

title: Have Here
description:
Pete and company came over about a month ago after a quiet set at this year's Hillstock. A neighbor came by at the end of the night and made desperate pleas for an end to the weekend long music marathon. We were able to cram this recording in right before Pete went off to Philadelphia and I left for 6 weeks. He is currently playing with Laura Stevenson and the Cans on their current tour with Maps & Atlases and Cults.

This song is off of their recent full length which you can download for FREE from [Quote Unquote Records](http://www.quoteunquoterecords.com/qur039.htm).

Apologies to anyone waiting for all the other pink couches that I have been filmed. This one was recorded without the use of my field recorder, so it was easier to get up right away. Stay tuned, I'm back all summer!

title: Since You Left
description:
Kate's second time on the Pink Couch, but it could have been her third if she joined Nate for the [Used Kids recording](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/used-kids/true-blue-rocker/).

Big Eyes has a new record out on [Evil Weevil Records](http://www.evilweevilrecords.com/). They also recently sold out of tape version on Ride the Fury. The band includes two members of the recently defunct [Used Kids](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/author/used-kids/), and Mark Bronzino of Kicking Spit/Seasick.

title: Song Against Ian Raymond
description:
One of the earliest Pink Couches we've ever recorded (10am!).

Mikey stopped by on his way out of town and quickly one-take-snaked this jam. You can find it on his recent release "42,069 Seconds With... ERGQUIST" under the moniker Ergquist. Ergquist is made up of Mikey and Marisa Bergquist of Besties fame.

Mikey also plays or has played in all of your favorite bands, including The Ergs, The Dopamines, Psyched to Die, Short Attention, The Used Kids, House Boat and millions more.

title: Kira
description:
I could make up some ridiculous last minute paragraph to describe the session or the band, but I would rather just repost their well written bio.

"It's a King Thing is from the Haddons, the Hills and the Heights of suburban Philadelphia. Their music can be defined as rock songs with a pop sensibility. Influences include The Lemonheads, the first two Weezer albums, the first two Weston albums and a bunch of tons more. Our band's songs can beat up your band's songs."

I had a lot of fun recording with these guys. Sorry it took 6 months to go up! I'm the worst at this.

title: Buddy Holly
description:
This is from last night's BTMI Weezer cover show. People wanted me to film the whole night, but I looked around and saw enough cameras doing that exact thing.

Congratulations to Jeff and company for pulling this off. It was pretty much flawless, with all the guitar solos and falsetto voices that Weezer is known for.

title: Something That I Need
description: This song is off their newest album "My Roaring 20s". They are currently on their way down to this year's Fest in Gainesville. You've heard of them already, this isn't anything new. This band and its 3 members are awesome.

title: which one of you assholes  ate christmas!!!
description:
Aimee was in town a couple of months ago for the yearly Big She-Bang. This was another of my "last minute" Pink Couches, I had emailed her one day previously and we were luckily able to figure out something for the next day. ( Note: I think I say "luckily" in 50% of my Pink Couch postings )

Des Ark is currently on tour in Europe for the next couple of months and returns to the U.S. for a 5 week tour with Pygmy Lush. They also have a new album, "Don't rock the boat, sink the fucker" coming out in January on Lovitt Records.

This is just what I needed to get me out of my posting funk. Check out the color grading! This song/session came out great. One of my favorites.

title: Stargazer
description: Recorded in May of 2010, GIM lugged that big green bass up the stairs, plopped it down and belted out two awesome songs. You can download their full length "Our Hearts are Gold, Our Grass is Blue" for a donation at [greenlandismelting.com/](http://www.greenlandismelting.com) or buy it on vinyl from Paper + Plastick.

title: RSVP
description:
Fake Problems are back with the rest of the band this time, and get to be the guinea pigs for the brand new camera set up. They have a brand new record out called "Real Ghost Caught On Tape" released by Side One Dummy Records.

Check them out on their US/Canadian tour that starts December 1st.

title: Speech Patterns
description:
Toby came by this past summer and recorded this gem off of his full length album "On Moving And Standing Still". You can buy it off his [website](http://tobyfoster.terrorware.com/â€‹music)â€‹. I've always been impressed with Toby's guitar playing. It's tight and crisp, which stands out in a sea of jangly folk.

*\*I am an awful writer. Listen to this please.*

title: Hotel Bibles
description:
These Torontonians ( that was fun to type ) stopped by a couple of months ago after our birthday party for Pat. I completely forget what they did while they were here. I think it was the show at the Knitting Factory...yeah that's it. The sound there was horrendous, so I missed out on videos from everyone.

This song is the title track off of their release Hotel Bibles. You can download their follow-up [self-titled 7"](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/junior-battles/self-titled/) from If You Make It.

title: Ravenswood
description:
This is Evan's second time on the Pink Couch, but his first time on the site. He's one of the unlucky few who decided to come over during the Summer of Zero Updates. When I heard that he was going to be back in town, I jumped at the chance to re-record him using the new setup. [Koji](www.myspace.com/kojisaysaloha) also recorded a session the same day, which actually worked out timing wise.

They both recently recorded a split together for [No Sleep Records](http://nosleeprecs.com/release.php?id=66), which you can pre-order right now.

title: Platforms
description:
We have a tie for "Most People on the Couch" with Selfish Whales matching Ava Luna with 7 people crammed in. This session was recorded during Hillstock this year when we snuck back to my apartment before their set. The band was also one of the last loud bands to play Hillstock this year, when a next door neighbor came by and complained.

You can download their full length "[Enjoy The Weather](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/selfish-whales/enjoy-the-weather/)" from this very site!

title: Everything We Need
description: Witt and crew came by the same weekend as [Selfish Whales](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/selfish-whales/platforms/), and I think putting these two Pink Couches together really works well. The Wild recently released their sophomore effort "Set Ourselves Free" which you can buy from [Asian Man Records](http://www.asianmanrecords.com/index.php) or download from [Quote Unquote Records](http://quoteunquoterecords.com/qur040.htm). How many records can boast being produced and recorded by Jeff Rosenstock and Joe Queer?

title: Small
description:
This is from last nights show at 538 Johnson. I was a little worried about the sound, but everything turned out awesome. This is off of their full length Weight of Air. These 4 dudes are currently on tour, so check their website to see if they are heading your way.

*\*I ran out of time and just threw this together. Toby Foster's Pink Couch will be going up tomorrow. Promise!*

title: Our First Brains
description:
Barnett and co. came by over the summer despite some heavy colds going around. For some reason I refused to clean up the "set" of the pink couch. Now I can't zoom in because everyone is basically playing to the edge. My first reaction was to cut it up and make it look like the Superchunk video for "Art Class".

That idea didn't last long.

Pick up their recent release "World Record" which was released this year by [Barsuk Records](http://www.barsuk.com/).

title: The Healthy One
description:
This was filmed at the Knitting Factory during Jeff Rosenstock's Holiday Charity Event Spectacular. The show was tons of fun except when Jeff called me out for giving him a weird look. I'm too tall for my own good.

LS+tC get better and better with each show, and I'm excited to hear their new record. This song will be on their upcoming Don Giovanni release, which should be out sometime next year.

title: Real Mean
description:
This was actually the joke recording of the set, and we recorded two other takes with Erica. Unfortunately, the camera kept overheating and we ran out of time. Luckily we have this gem to tide you over until the RVIVR crew comes back to the east coast.

Great people, great band.

Download all of their records at [http://www.rumbletowne.com/](http://www.rumbletowne.com/). Their newest EP "Dirty Water" is amazing.

title: Giants Sleeping
description:
This was supposed to go back to back with [Into It Over It's session](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/into-it-over-it/ravenswood/), but I ran into some trouble finding certain parts of the recording. This song is off of his 12 inch split with IIOI on [No Sleep Records](http://nosleepstore.com/). I recently got a copy of the record and I can attest to how great all these songs are. Pick it up now.

IYMI KOJI IIOI

title: You're Alright
description:
I met Giles after Calvinball stopped by to record a Pink Couch. It was right before Fest, during CMJ, so tons of bands were in town, including a bunch of random people from the UK. After going to some shows/parties together, someone told me that I should do film a Cynics Pink Couch. I can't remember if I even listened to his music prior to the recording or just took the person's word. In the end it didn't matter because the session came out great.

His record 'Stones I've Thrown' is avalable as a free download on [MediaFire](http://www.mediafire.com/?jm2nnmz3zkm) and on 7" vinyl through [Happy Days Records](http://happydaysrecords.bigcartel.com/).

*\*Clare informs me that Giles was the someone who brought up a Cynics Pink Couch. I think I had one too many white russians/four lokos/random beers that night. Barf.*

*\*\*I was recently informed that Calvinball actually influenced this session. I'll believe anything at this point.*

title: Great Northern Diver
description:
Another large group on the three seater couch. Whenever there are large groups I usually have a hard time fitting in the percussion. Half the time the drummer looks likes they're playing on air. This is no exception.

Summer People have a [Demo EP](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/â€‹album/â€‹summer-people/â€‹demo/â€‹) on IYMI.

They also have a full length, Good Problems, available on [Red Leader Records](http://redleaderrecords.com/â€‹).

title: Dancing With Myself
description:
Another video from the crypt! In 2008, Frances knocked a Billy Idol cover out of the park.

It's really weird to see the old setup and our multiple cameras. You can tell that I've never took a video class, especially when editing between a stationary camera and a handheld.

Enjoy this relic!

title: Western Apathy
description:
This week's installment of the Pink Couch Sessions features Richmond's The Two Funerals. They came by last summer with a bunch of folks who were on tour and we ate some bagels and recorded a couple different groups at the same time. I think we had fun, right? I think having NY bagels makes anyone happy.

TTF have an LP coming out on [Rorshach Records](http://www.rorschachrecords.net/) in the near future.

title: Paycheck
description:
Did you know that typing "Fucking Cops" in google brings you right to their album download page on ifyoumakeit? That is completely crazy, people must say/type "fucking cops" all of the time. The 4th link of the page is to an article detailing "5 Ways to Fuck With Cops".

This is off of their 2010 album "You Have The Right to Shut The Fuck Up".

title: American Tour Part Four
description:
Day 4 of the 2010 American War tour. Rock Island, IL. More to come soon!

Songs include.
American War-Fanning Flames

Listen to the rest of this session over at daytrotter.com

title: Watching Television
description:
Recorded in the summer of 2010 after the Hillstock festival. I think they were going for some sort of Human Centipede thing with the hair. That's weird right? Right?

They have a record out on [Blood of Jesus Records](http://bloodofjesusrecords.com/) called "Cult Classics".

*"no man. we are avataring. from avatar." — Wen-Jay Ying*
*"and i will continue to send you death threats until you say i sent you death threats on the website" — Wen-Jay Ying*

title: The Forgetting of Names and Faces
description:
This is the second time the Hendricks brothers have sat upon our glorious couch. They completely missed the second generation of cameras we had and skipped right to the age of the over-heating DSLR. Now a quartet, Annabel is currently releasing an EP on Tiny Engines, entitled "[Here We Are Tomorrow](http://www.tinyengines.net/releases/here-we-are-tomorrow/)". This is the first track off of that release.

You can also download their first EP, [Now That We're Alive](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/annabel/now-that-we-re-alive/), off of IYMI.

title: I Like Cats
description:
PJ from Grabass Charlestons just emailed me a second ago inquiring about this nameless video on Vimeo. I had forgotten to put this up and had somehow gotten halfway there.

This is from Fest 8? 7? It's kind of ridiculous how old this is, but it's definitely from the second camera era.

"I Like Cats" is off their 2007 split with The Ergs, which was released by No Idea Records.

title: Green Light, Go!
description:
THF came by with fellow Ohioans [Annabel](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/author/annabel/) about a month ago. This song is off of their upcoming full length "House Parts" which should be coming out really soon on [Sidejar Records](http://www.sidejarrecords.com/â€‹). You can hear two songs at their [Facebook page](http://www.facebook.com/â€‹pages/â€‹Two-Hand-Fools/â€‹111938775493603).

I think I'm getting the hang of this new camera. [Calvinball](http://www.myspace.com/thisiscalvinball) is up next!

title: Brighter Lights
description:
The band came all the way over here from the United Kingdom just to do this. Can you believe it? I can't. Mostly because it's not true, and they stopped by before they headed down south for last year's Fest in Gainesville. Jiro Marzano served as the escort for these lovely chaps during their stay in our fair city.

This song is off of their 2009 release, "Moving Homes and Breaking Bone", which you can download from their [bandcamp account](http://calvinball.bandcamp.com/) for free. I'm excited to play with them in the next two weeks when Thousandaires hop over for a few dates in the U.K.

title: Our Vessel
description:
NFAW had the (dis)pleasure of dealing with my incredibly finicky camera. It was constantly shutting off in the middle of recordings, and it forced me to stand behind it the whole time. I was basically crammed against the wall staring at 3 inch screen, begging it too not shut off. Each take was maddening as I crossed my fingers hoping it wouldn't shut off. Despite all of that nonsense, the guys were able to pull off an awesome take at exactly the right time.

NFAW currently have an LP titled "Don't Die" out on Academy Fight Song. AFS is also releasing a new 7" by the band, so stay tuned for that too. "Don't Die" is an awesome record that you should pick up if you get the chance. FFO The Get Up Kids and Ninja Gun.

title: Be Three Years Old
description:
This was recorded many years ago, before this song even had a name. I don't think Perch Patchwork was even done. I just found it now!

Archiving all the old videos onto YouTube today, so maybe I'll find a couple more gems!

title: Landlocked State
description:
I first found out about TCK through a compilation for 1919 Hemphill, an awesome venue in Ft. Worth, TX. We tried to work out a Pink Couch a couple of times, but something always came up. Luckily we were able to remedy that on their recent trip to Brooklyn.

Their most recent LP is 2009's Foreign Lands, which was released by [Protagonist Music](http://www.protagonistmusic.com/site/index.php). Lead singer, Andrew Savage, also moved to the Brooklyn area, so hopefully this doesn't mean the end of Teenage Cool Kids.

title: The Rind
description: After tons of problems involving my crazy camera we were finally able to get Angie, Marilyn and Joe up on this website. This is off their demo which you can download from this very website. Definitely check this band out if you get a chance, they write such catchy, awesome songs that get stuck in your head.

title: Crosby Sucks
description:
This is off of their recent full-length "Never Settle", which is being released by Traffic Street and Toxic Pop Records. Carl wouldn't wear his bass gloves while playing, so I was extremely mad the whole time I filmed it. They also brought this little dweeb with them who refused to grow a mustache. I am so frustrated.

You can also download their first album "[First Six Songs](ifyoumakeit.com/â€‹album/â€‹sundials/â€‹first-six-songs/â€‹)" from IYMI.

title: Tablecloth
description:
I spent the last 45 minutes trying to search for the name of this song. I scoured the internet, checking each of their releases and all of their lyrics. I was completely at a loss when I finally decided to just type their lyrics into Google. Lo and behold, it came up as a Paul Baribeau song! Somehow I had forgotten that they had done a cover.

This song is off Paul's self-titled record which you can get from [Plan-It-X Records](http://www.plan-it-x.org/release/paul-baribeau/self-titled/). Chewing on Tinfoil's most recent release is the Shaving CD, which you can get from [Asbestos Records](http://www.asbestosrecords.bigcartel.com/). You can also download their full length "Get Rich or Try Dyin'" from [Quote Unquote Records](http://quoteunquoterecords.com/qur033.htm) for free/donation.

title: You Guys Are Gonna Wake Up My Mom
description:
Field Mouse were a group of people that existed within my circle of friends but I don't think we were ever in the same place. Mathematically, it seemed that we would eventually run into each other and when I did, I was excited to invite them over to record.

Field Mouse are currently working on an EP to be released this summer. There has also been some talk of a split with [Very Okay](http://quoteunquoterecords.com/qur048.htm). Someone needs to put that out!

You can also download their 2010 record "You Are Here" from [Bandcamp](http://fieldmouse.bandcamp.com/album/you-are-here). Donate!

title: Spooky, Scary
description:
These doofuses just released one of the best records of the year and everyone hates them for it. Well I do, and the word "best" is subjective.

They took 103 takes to film this. This is the best they could do.

Download their record "[Funtitled](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/â€‹album/â€‹spraynard/â€‹funtitled/â€‹)" from IYMI for free and buy it from [Asian Man Records](http://www.asianmanrecords.com) May 10th.

title: Wild Eyes
description: Paul first recorded a Pink Couch about 2 years ago on the orange Fest couch. This time we were able to get him over to record one of his own songs. You can download this and 9 more songs on his LP "[Unbearable](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/paul-baribeau/unbearable/)" from this site!

title: Blink 182 Reunion
description:
Filmed by [Flipswitch Films](http://flipswitchfilms.com) at last year's [Hillstock](http://neverbreakdown.com/hillstock/). Come out this June 3rd/4th/5th for more awesome bands playing in my neighborhood, Clinton Hill, Brooklyn. It's tons of fun, and this year it'll have a block party!

I think they captured every ridiculous face I make. I'm pretty sure the first one was because Jeff didn't play the dead notes. Being up that high is weird, especially alone.

You can still download the EP, *[Lando](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/kudrow/lando/)*, that this is off from this very site. We also have a new split with CA's Hard Girls coming out on Asian Man this June. You can hear a song off it on our [Tumblr](http://kudrow.tumblr.com/post/2897893950/kudrow-boo).

---

*Kudrow play their song, "Blink 182 Reunion" at Day 2 of Hillstock Music Festival in a backyard in Brooklyn, NY.

June 12, 2010

*

*Shot by: Jonathan E Rotberg and Matt Klammer
Edited by: Eric J. Williams and Jonathan E Rotberg
Color by: Jonathan E Rotberg
Audio recorded and mixed by: Spaceman Sound*

title: American Tour Part Five
description:
Day 5 of the 2010 American War tour. Columbus,OH. More to come soon!

Songs include.
American War-Rolling
Vialka-Everyone's Talking (No One's Listening)

Vialka is an amazing band from France. And the little girl featured is their amazing daughter. Please check them out @ [http://vialka.com/](http://vialka.com/)

title: The Mamas and the Papas
description:
David came by about a little while ago on the same crazy day that Paul and Kari came through. This time through he decided to leave the couch undisturbed.

Currently David is on tour traveling the U.S. See if he's coming to your town at his website [I Love You This Is A Robbery](http://iloveyouthisisarobbery.com/shows/) and pre-order his new record, *"The Papas"* from [Plan-It-X](http://www.plan-it-x.org/release/spoonboy/the-papas-lp-version-/).

title: Future Nun
description:
Kari stopped by with good buddy [Paul Baribeau](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/search/paul+baribeau/) about 2 months ago and recorded this jam. You can also download her record "Brain Cloud" from [Bandcamp](http://theboywhocouldfly.bandcamp.com/) for a donation or buy it on CD.

*Sorry it took so long to get the PCS back on track. Stay tuned for more.*

title: Church Street in Ruins
description:
From our first show in Brighton, UK on the State Lottery/Bangers/El Morgan tour. Thanks to Jay for setting the show up. Expect more videos from the rest of this tour.

This is off their newest LP, "Small Pleasures", co-released by [Specialist Subject Records](http://shop.specialistsubjectrecords.co.uk/store) and [Kiss of Death Records](http://kissofdeath.limitedpressing.com/products/9356). You can also download the record from their [Bandcamp](http://bangersbangers.bandcamp.com/).

title: Beasts
description: A song entitled "Beasts" from Saintseneca's upcoming album "Last". Due out August 2011. It was filmed by Jon Washington. Check out his American War tour footage in the [Series section](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/category/series).

title: Cut the Cord
description: RVIVR is one of those bands that tours so much it's crazy. After the fiasco of their last PCS, I'm glad they were able to come back so soon. This is off their self-titled record, which you can download from [Rumbletowne Records](http://www.rumbletowne.com/rtr/rtr-releases/RTR-009).

title: Life Boat
description:
This video is from the 10 year anniversary of [Square of Opposition Records](http://www.squareofopposition.com/) Festival which was held in Bethlehem, PA last weekend. It was an all-day celebration of Lehigh Valley music, featuring extinct bands like Hypatia, [Yo Man Go](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/yo-man-go/discography/), Audioheart, Government Satire and Robot Attack, who were summoned from the dead by the wizard Chris Reject. A lot of the bands that played that weekend are on his record label, so make sure to stop by SoO and check out his releases. He's been doing this for 10 years, which has to count for something. Right?

Audioheart is one of the many bands Charlie Cottone has been in while living in the valley. You could tell this from the amount of times he was up on stage on Saturday. It was sort of ridiculous. Charlie also plays in Bear Hug (formerly Paramedic). I will not mention anything about Dave, Ryan or Flem, mostly because I don't really know Flem. You can [download their 2003 Demo from MediaFire](http://www.mediafire.com/?c435s8s9h4xjx85).

title: Bathtub
description:
I'm currently in Paris right now , connected to the worst wi-fi signal in Europe. It keeps going out and I have to restart my computer constantly. Despite these inconveniences, I was able to upload and post this amazing video from Katie's new solo project, Waxahatchee. I think this came out great and if you like it, head over to her blog by following the link to the right. You can download her full length for free! "Bathtub" is on their too!

If you like this then also make sure to download [P.S. Eliot's *Sadie*](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/ps-eliot/sadie/).

title: Nothing Left
description:
The recording of this session has been the focus of many emails back and forth between Allison and me. Despite our busy summers, we were finally able to figure out a day to record this.

"Nothing Left" is off her new record, which she is currently working on. Check her website to see crazy pictures of her tracking setup and fun studio pictures.

*I'm currently writing this while driving through the Scottish hillside. Sheep!*

title: Mr. Mystery
description:
This was recorded at the same time as The Sidekicks' session. It was great hanging out with these guys all day, and to see them play again. I'm extremely stoked for Matt's new American War record, which will hopefully be coming out soon.

For now, pick up his LP "Rhetoric" on [Shout Out Loud Records](http://www.shoutoutloudprints.com/store/index.php?route=product/product&product_id=51) or [download it from IYMI](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/american-war/rhetoric/).

title: I Saw The Wolf
description:
Somehow I've never seen this band live, despite loving everything they've put out over the last couple of years. I keep missing them play over and over, it's almost annoying at this point. Luckily for me, I was able to drag them over to my stinky pink couch and force them to play in a hot unbearable apartment.

This song is off of their recent full length, "Two Worlds", on Run for Cover Records. Pick it up or check this band out when they come through your town. They are incredibly nice and thought that this was fun.

Crazy kids! It's not fun!

title: Failed Invasion
description:
They came, they drank Jeremiah Weed, they played, they played again, and they left. DH! just released their full length LP, Domesplitter, on [Kind of Like Records](http://kindoflikerecords.com/releases/domesplitter).

You can hear all their music at their [Bandcamp](http://directhit.bandcamp.com/)!

title: Soups Whatever
description:
We did this whole session in 20 minutes. It was redonkulous. I think I had to be somewhere, or maybe they did. Oh yea, I had practice. I think I ended up being early anyway. I pointlessly rushed them.

SORRY [MIXTAPES](http://mixtapesohio.bandcamp.com/)!

title: Hiding Out
description:
In honor of [Ramsey's comic post](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/comic/ramsey-beyer/an-evening-with-evan-weiss/), I decided to post this great Pink Couch Session of her buddies from Washington DC, The Ambulars. This was recorded a couple of months ago before their show at the Manifesta Loft. The song is off their upcoming full length which they are recording in a month.

You can also download all of their records from their website. Check it out if you are a fan of Superchunk, The Anniversary and other such things.

title: 1940's Fighter Jet
description:
In honor of their newest release "Awkward Breeds", we have a brand new Pink Couch from Ohio's The Sidekicks.

I can attest to the fact that their new record is amazing, and you should all do yourselves a favor and preorder the new record. You can order it on vinyl from [Really Records](http://reallyrecords.bigcartel.com/category/really-records) and on CD from [Red Scare Industries](http://redscare.storenvy.com/) along with their "Grace" 7 inch.

Check them out on their upcoming [March tour](http://sidekicksohio.tumblr.com/shows) with [Cheap Girls](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/author/cheap-girls/) when they swing through your town.

title: Sure Thing
description:
Fat Shadow consists of members of The Door-Keys, Landlord and Pink Razors. They came by so long ago, that they probably forgot about doing it, or have been annoyed at me for a long time.

You can listen to their newest album "Foot of Love" at their [Bandcamp](http://fatshadow.bandcamp.com/). This song is featured on there.

You can also download Erin's "[Booyah Grandma Demo](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/album/erin-tobey/booyah-grandma-demo/)" from IYMI! HOW CONVENIENT!

title: Hope It's Not A Deposit Bottle!
description:
Another video from the archives that I archived wrong and thought I lost. This is off their 2010 release "[The Past In Present](http://youblewit.bandcamp.com/)". These guys are pretty cool. Are they all still in the band? Their next record is going to be on Top Shelf Records! They're also be on tour during March 2012 w/ Direct Effect.

FFO American Football but happier.

title: Missing Dogs
description:
Another video from the ON THE ROAD series on IYMI. This session is one of my favorites on the whole site. You can currently hear this song on their 2011 release "Last" on [Mama Bird Recording Co](http://mamabirdrecordingco.com/).

They also have a Daytrotter session coming out this week, so make sure you check it out.

This was filmed in The Fort or The Boneyard in Brooklyn. I can't remember which one is which! Thanks to everyone at the house for letting us stop by and do this.

\*Yes, that's Steve from [The Sidekicks](http://www.ifyoumakeit.com/video/the-sidekicks/1940s-fighter-jet/)

title: "Last" Episode Two
description:
In December of 2010 Saintseneca entered the studio to record their first full length album. This is a documentation of the time spent in the studio.

Their album "Last" is not out on [Mama Bird Recording Co](http://mamabirdrecordingco.com/).

This was film at [The Tone Shoppe](http://www.thetoneshoppe.com/) in Columbus Ohio.

The song featured in this episode is called "Acid Rain".

Filmed and editing by Jon Washington.

title: Young and Happy!
description:
From their recent show at Death By Audio with These Days, Delay and Nude Beach. This is off their newest album "[Get Disowned](http://hopalong.bandcamp.com/album/get-disowned)."

On tour now!

title: I'm Afraid Of Everything
description:
This day was one of my favorites in recent memory and it was a total blast. Thanks to Bob and Chris for hanging around while my band sound-checked. They are currently playing a couple of shows around the U.S. and from twitter, it seems like they may be working on new music!

If you caught them on this recent tour, you know how awesome it was. Hearing the intro of "The New Nathan Detroits" live was amazing.

They also recorded an acoustic version of "A Dozen Roses", which I will release later in September. Enjoy!

(Thanks to Tito and Lisa for setting this up)

title: Forever Got Shorter
description: Live from Bowery Ballroom on their recent Frame & Canvas tour. Stay tuned next week for an acoustic session from Bob and Chris!

title: Love Is Not Enough
description:
This was recorded at Bowery Ballroom along with Braid's session. Mike was amazing especially for jumping in so quickly and with little notice.

You wouldn't be able to tell but this was done during sound check before the show that night. It was extremely hectic trying to cram everyone between checks, so thank you to Bowery Ballroom and Aficionado for being cool with this random thing.

This is brand new song which doesn't appear on any of his records. You can check out his upcoming tour dates and listen to all his music at his [Bandcamp page](http://owenmusic.bandcamp.com).

Thanks to Chase for setting this up!

title: Arizona
description: Recorded in the too far distant past! This is off their amazing record "Baseball Season" which came out this year. Apologies on the lack of updates! I'm strugglin' here!

title: Big Bird
description: From their tour with Laura Stevenson & The Cans and ROAR. Both bands join AJJ on stage for the last song of the night and the tour! I wish I had better audio but who cares! This was fun.

title: Bloody Lips
description:
Recorded a long time ago in a far off land which I call my apartment. This song is off of a 7" of the same name, which you can hear here.

[http://zebu.bandcamp.com/album/bloody-lips-7](http://zebu.bandcamp.com/album/bloody-lips-7)

title: Wait, Move
description:
This should have been on the site 2 years ago, but it quickly got lost on my mess of a hard drive. There's a couple of other sessions from 2011's Weapons of Mass Creation Fest that will be going up as I find them. Thanks to Jeff Finley for inviting me that year and all the buddies who hung out. Sorry for the ridiculous delay!

This song is off of Max's recent record "Aging Truths". You can hear it here. [http://meridianohio.bandcamp.com/](http://meridianohio.bandcamp.com/)

Check here for info on the 4th Annual WMC Fest in Cleveland this year!
[http://wmcfest.com/](http://wmcfest.com/)

Max also plays in Signals Midwest.
[http://www.signalsmidwest.tumblr.com](http://www.signalsmidwest.tumblr.com)

title: Bullshit Stories
description:
This is the story of LCO vs my spectacular flu that took up three weeks of my life. I had to cancel on this pink couch which was a total bummer for me considering how far away they live. Luckily I was able to pitch the idea of them recording it on their own and sending it my way.

I think this actually looks better than 90% of the content on this site. Oh, I'm so embarrassed. I wish there was a hole I could just crawl into and die.

"Ok, throw him in the hole."

*Check out their bandcamp and recent split with Three Man Cannon by clicking the link to the right! Thanks again to the band for setting this up and figuring everything out. Apologies for the non-pinkness of the couch.*

title: No One Else
description:
From a 2010 Bomb the Music Industry! Weezer cover show. People wanted me to film the whole night, but I looked around and saw enough cameras doing that exact thing.

Congratulations to Jeff and company for pulling this off. It was pretty much flawless, with all the guitar solos and falsetto voices that Weezer is known for.

title: For Things That Haven't Come Yet
description: Nana Grizol at the Cake Shop in Manhattan in 2008. Crappy quality, but I realized I had this set to private forever.
